{{神社| 名称=飛幡八幡宮| 所在地=福岡県北九州市戸畑区浅生2丁目2-2| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|53|36|N|130|49|38|E|scale:30000}}| 祭神=神功皇后
応神天皇
須佐之男命
比売大神
道祖神| 社格=県社・別表神社| 創建=[[建久]]年間| 例祭=[[10月14日]]| 神事=戸畑祇園大山笠行事
放生会}} '''飛幡八幡宮'''（とばたはちまんぐう）は、[[福岡県]][[北九州市]][[戸畑区]]にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]で、現在は[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。 ==祭神== [[八幡神]]（[[神功皇后]]・[[応神天皇]]）・[[比売神|比売大神]]・[[スサノオ|須佐之男命]]・[[道祖神]]を祀る。 ==歴史== [[建久]]年間（[[1190年]] - [[1199年]]）、筑前の[[宇都宮氏]]（[[麻生氏]]）の祖である[[宇都宮重業]]が、出身地の宇都宮から[[氏神]]の八幡神を勧請し、[[花尾城]]の[[鬼門]]にあたる枝光村宮田山に祀ったのに始まる。後に戸畑村に遷され、戸畑・中原両村の[[産土神]]とされた。江戸時代には藩主[[黒田家]]の[[祈願所]]とされた。大正9年に現在地の浅生に遷座した。昭和6年に県社に列した。平成7年、それまでの「戸畑八幡神社」から「飛幡八幡宮」に社名を変更した。 ==祭事== [[7月21日]] - [[7月23日|23日]]に行われる[[戸畑祇園大山笠行事]]は、当社および区内の菅原神社・中原八幡宮の祭りで、国の[[重要無形民俗文化財]]となっている。『筑前国続風土記』によれば、[[享和]]2年（[[1802年]]）、戸畑村内に疫病が蔓延し、飛幡八幡宮の須佐之男命に平癒を祈願した所終息したため、翌享和3年（[[1803年]]）から始められたものである。 [[Category:福岡県の神社|とはたはちまんくう]] [[Category:府県社|とはたはちまんくう]] [[Category:別表神社|とはたはちまんくう]] {{shinto-stub}}

放生会}} '''飛幡八幡宮'''（とびはたはちまんぐう）は、[[福岡県]][[北九州市]][[戸畑区]]にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]で、現在は[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。 == 祭神 == [[八幡神]]（[[神功皇后]]・[[応神天皇]]）・[[比売神|比売大神]]・[[スサノオ|須佐之男命]]・[[道祖神]]を祀る。 == 歴史 == [[建久]]年間（[[1190年]] - [[1199年]]）、筑前の[[宇都宮氏]]（[[麻生氏]]）の祖である[[宇都宮重業]]が、出身地の宇都宮から[[氏神]]の八幡神を勧請し、[[花尾城]]の[[鬼門]]にあたる枝光村宮田山に祀ったのに始まる。後に戸畑村に遷され、戸畑・中原両村の[[産土神]]とされた。江戸時代には藩主[[黒田氏|黒田家]]の[[祈願所]]とされた。[[1920年]]（大正9年）に現在地の[[浅生 (北九州市)|浅生]]に遷座した。[[1931年]]（昭和6年）に県社に列した。[[1995年]]（平成7年）、それまでの「戸畑八幡神社」から「飛幡八幡宮」に社名を変更した。 == 祭事 == 7月第4週金・土・日に行われる[[戸畑祇園大山笠]]は、当社および区内の菅原神社・中原八幡宮の祭りで、国の[[重要無形民俗文化財]]となっている。『筑前国続風土記』によれば、[[享和]]2年（[[1802年]]）、戸畑村内に疫病が蔓延し、飛幡八幡宮の[[須佐之男命]]に平癒を祈願した所終息したため、翌享和3年（[[1803年]]）から始められたものである。 {{DEFAULTSORT:とひはたはちまんくう}} [[Category:福岡県の神社]] [[Category:福岡県の旧県社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:八幡宮]] [[Category:戸畑区]] {{shinto-stub}}

放生会}} '''飛幡八幡宮'''（とびはたはちまんぐう）は、[[福岡県]][[北九州市]][[戸畑区]]にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]で、現在は[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。 == 祭神 == [[八幡神]]（[[神功皇后]]・[[応神天皇]]）・[[比売神|比売大神]]・[[スサノオ|須佐之男命]]・[[道祖神]]を祀る。 == 歴史 == [[建久]]年間（[[1190年]] - [[1199年]]）、筑前の[[宇都宮氏]]（[[麻生氏]]）の祖である[[宇都宮重業]]が、出身地の宇都宮から[[氏神]]の八幡神を勧請し、[[花尾城]]の[[鬼門]]にあたる枝光村宮田山に祀ったのに始まる。後に戸畑村に遷され、戸畑・中原両村の[[産土神]]とされた。江戸時代には藩主[[黒田氏|黒田家]]の[[祈願所]]とされた。[[1920年]]（大正9年）に現在地の[[浅生 (北九州市)|浅生]]に遷座した。[[1931年]]（昭和6年）に県社に列した。[[1995年]]（平成7年）、それまでの「戸畑八幡神社」から「飛幡八幡宮」に社名を変更した。 == 祭事 == 7月第4週金・土・日に行われる[[戸畑祇園大山笠]]は、当社および区内の[[菅原神社 (北九州市戸畑区)|菅原神社]]・中原八幡宮の祭りで、国の[[重要無形民俗文化財]]となっている。『筑前国続風土記』によれば、[[享和]]2年（[[1802年]]）、戸畑村内に疫病が蔓延し、飛幡八幡宮の[[須佐之男命]]に平癒を祈願した所終息したため、翌享和3年（[[1803年]]）から始められたものである。 == ギャラリー == {{gallery |ファイル:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_01.jpg|一の鳥居 |ファイル:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_03.jpg|正一位稲荷大明神 }} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とひはたはちまんくう}} [[Category:福岡県の神社]] [[Category:福岡県の旧県社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:八幡宮]] [[Category:戸畑区の歴史]] [[Category:北九州市の建築物]]

revision.text:
[[sv:Tobihata-hachimangū]] [[ja:飛幡八幡宮]] {{Shrine |Name= Tobihata Hachimangu |Image=[[File:Tobihata Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_02.jpg|300px]] |Location= 2-2-2 Asano, Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu, Fukuoka Prefecture |Position= {{Coord|33|53|36|N|130|49|38|E|region:JP-40_scale:30000|display=inline,title}} |Deity= {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}
{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}}
{{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神}}
{{ill|Dōsojin|ja|道祖神}} |Rank= Prefectural Shrine, Special Shrine |Founded= Kenkyū period (1190-1199) |Annual festival= October 14 |Rituals= Tobata Gion Yamakasa Event
Hōjōe }} '''Tobihata Hachimangu''' (とびはたはちまんぐう{{ill|Tobihata Hachimangu|ja|飛幡八幡宮}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu|ja|戸畑区}}, {{ill|Kitakyushu|ja|北九州市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}. It was formerly ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} and is currently listed as a {{ill|Special Shrine|ja|別表神社}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}} ({{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} and {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}), {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}}, and {{ill|Dōsojin|ja|道祖神}}. == History == In the Kenkyū period (1190-1199), {{ill|Utsunomiya Shigeie|ja|宇都宮重業}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏}} ({{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏}}), brought the Hachiman deity from his hometown of Utsunomiya and enshrined it in Miyata Mountain in Edamitsu Village, which was the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門}} (northeast) of {{ill|Hanao Castle|ja|花尾城}}. Later, the shrine was moved to Tobata Village and became the guardian deity of Tobata and Nakahara Villages. During the Edo period, it was designated a prayer site by the {{ill|Kuroda clan|ja|黒田氏}}. In 1920, it was relocated to its current site in Asano. In 1931, it was designated a Prefectural Shrine. In 1995, the shrine's name was changed from "Tobata Hachiman Shrine" to "Tobihata Hachimangu." == Festivals == The {{ill|Tobata Gion Yamakasa|ja|戸畑祇園大山笠}} festival, held on the fourth weekend of July, is a major event at the shrine and at {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu)|ja|菅原神社 (北九州市戸畑区)}} and Nakahara Hachiman Shrine in the ward. It is designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} of Japan. According to the {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記}}, the festival began in Kyōwa 2 (1802) when an epidemic broke out in Tobata Village. After praying for healing at the shrine to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}}, the epidemic ceased, and the festival was started the following year. == Gallery == {{gallery |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_01.jpg|First Torii |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_03.jpg|Shoichii Inari Daimyojin }} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tobihata Hachimangu}} [[Category:Special Shrines]] [[Category:Prefectural Shrines of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:History of Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]]

[[sv:Tobihata-hachimangū]] [[ja:飛幡八幡宮]] {{Shrine |Name= Tobihata Hachimangu |Image=[[File:Tobihata Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_02.jpg|300px]] |Location= 2-2-2 Asano, Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu, Fukuoka Prefecture |Position= {{Coord|33|53|36|N|130|49|38|E|region:JP-40_scale:30000|display=inline,title}} |Deity= {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}
{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}}
{{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神}}
{{ill|Dōsojin|ja|道祖神}} |Rank= Prefectural Shrine, Special Shrine |Founded= Kenkyū period (1190-1199) |Annual festival= October 14 |Rituals= Tobata Gion Yamakasa Event

Later, the shrine was moved to Tobata Village and became the guardian deity of Tobata and Nakahara Villages. During the Edo period, it was designated a prayer site by the {{ill|Kuroda clan|ja|黒田氏}}. In 1920, it was relocated to its current site in Asano. In 1931, it was designated a Prefectural Shrine. In 1995, the shrine's name was changed from "Tobata Hachiman Shrine" to "Tobihata Hachimangu." == Festivals == The {{ill|Tobata Gion Yamakasa|ja|戸畑祇園大山笠}} festival, held on the fourth weekend of July, is a major event at the shrine and at {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu)|ja|菅原神社 (北九州市戸畑区)}} and Nakahara Hachiman Shrine in the ward. It is designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} of Japan. According to the {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記}}, the festival began in Kyōwa 2 (1802) when an epidemic broke out in Tobata Village. After praying for healing at the shrine to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}}, the epidemic ceased, and the festival was started the following year. == Gallery == {{gallery |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_01.jpg|First Torii |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_03.jpg|Shoichii Inari Daimyojin }} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tobihata Hachimangu}} [[Category:Special Shrines]] [[Category:Prefectural Shrines of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:History of Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]]

{{ill|Dōsojin|ja|道祖神}} |Rank= Prefectural Shrine, Special Shrine |Founded= Kenkyū period (1190-1199) |Annual festival= October 14 |Rituals= Tobata Gion Yamakasa Event
Hōjōe }} '''Tobihata Hachimangu''' (とびはたはちまんぐう{{ill|Tobihata Hachimangu|ja|飛幡八幡宮}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu|ja|戸畑区}}, {{ill|Kitakyushu|ja|北九州市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}. It was formerly ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} and is currently listed as a {{ill|Special Shrine|ja|別表神社}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}} ({{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} and {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}), {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}}, and {{ill|Dōsojin|ja|道祖神}}. == History == In the Kenkyū period (1190-1199), {{ill|Utsunomiya Shigeie|ja|宇都宮重業}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏}} ({{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏}}), brought the Hachiman deity from his hometown of Utsunomiya and enshrined it in Miyata Mountain in Edamitsu Village, which was the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門}} (northeast) of {{ill|Hanao Castle|ja|花尾城}}. Later, the shrine was moved to Tobata Village and became the guardian deity of Tobata and Nakahara Villages. During the Edo period, it was designated a prayer site by the {{ill|Kuroda clan|ja|黒田氏}}. In 1920, it was relocated to its current site in Asano. In 1931, it was designated a Prefectural Shrine. In 1995, the shrine's name was changed from "Tobata Hachiman Shrine" to "Tobihata Hachimangu." == Festivals == The {{ill|Tobata Gion Yamakasa|ja|戸畑祇園大山笠}} festival, held on the fourth weekend of July, is a major event at the shrine and at {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu)|ja|菅原神社 (北九州市戸畑区)}} and Nakahara Hachiman Shrine in the ward. It is designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} of Japan. According to the {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記}}, the festival began in Kyōwa 2 (1802) when an epidemic broke out in Tobata Village. After praying for healing at the shrine to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}}, the epidemic ceased, and the festival was started the following year. == Gallery == {{gallery |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_01.jpg|First Torii |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_03.jpg|Shoichii Inari Daimyojin }} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tobihata Hachimangu}} [[Category:Special Shrines]] [[Category:Prefectural Shrines of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:History of Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:[[Category:Original pages]]]]

After praying for healing at the shrine to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}}, the epidemic ceased, and the festival was started the following year. == Gallery == {{gallery |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_01.jpg|First Torii |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_03.jpg|Shoichii Inari Daimyojin }} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tobihata Hachimangu}} [[Category:Special Shrines]] [[Category:Prefectural Shrines of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:History of Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:[[Category:Original pages]]]]

It is designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} of Japan. According to the {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記}}, the festival began in Kyōwa 2 (1802) when an epidemic broke out in Tobata Village. After praying for healing at the shrine to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}}, the epidemic ceased, and the festival was started the following year. == Gallery == {{gallery |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_01.jpg|First Torii |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_03.jpg|Shoichii Inari Daimyojin }} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tobihata Hachimangu}} [[Category:Beppyo Shrines]] [[Category:Prefectural Shrines of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:History of Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:[[Category:Original pages]]]]

{{神社 |名称= 飛幡八幡宮 |画像=[[ファイル:Tobihata Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_02.jpg|250px]]
拝殿 |所在地= [[福岡県]][[北九州市]][[戸畑区]][[浅生 (北九州市)|浅生]]2丁目2-2 |位置= {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|53|36.8|N|130|49|39|E|region:JP-40_scale:30000|display=inline,title}} |祭神= [[神功皇后]]
[[応神天皇]]
[[須佐之男命]]
[[比売大神]]
[[道祖神]] |社格= 旧[[県社]]・[[別表神社]] |創建= [[建久]]年間 |例祭= [[10月14日]] |神事= 戸畑祇園大山笠行事
放生会 |地図={{Location map|Japan Fukuoka|label=飛幡八幡宮|coordinates= {{coord2|33|53|36.8|N|130|49|39|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|53|36.8|N|130|49|39|E}}}} }} '''飛幡八幡宮'''（とびはたはちまんぐう）は、[[福岡県]][[北九州市]][[戸畑区]]にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]で、現在は[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。 == 祭神 == [[八幡神]]（[[神功皇后]]・[[応神天皇]]）・[[比売神|比売大神]]・[[スサノオ|須佐之男命]]・[[道祖神]]を祀る。 == 歴史 == [[建久]]年間（[[1190年]] - [[1199年]]）、筑前の[[宇都宮氏]]（[[麻生氏]]）の祖である[[宇都宮重業]]が、出身地の宇都宮から[[氏神]]の八幡神を勧請し、[[花尾城]]の[[鬼門]]にあたる枝光村宮田山に祀ったのに始まる。後に戸畑村に遷され、戸畑・中原両村の[[産土神]]とされた。江戸時代には藩主[[黒田氏|黒田家]]の[[祈願所]]とされた。[[1920年]]（大正9年）に現在地の[[浅生 (北九州市)|浅生]]に遷座した。[[1931年]]（昭和6年）に県社に列した。[[1995年]]（平成7年）、それまでの「戸畑八幡神社」から「飛幡八幡宮」に社名を変更した。 == 祭事 == 7月第4週金・土・日に行われる[[戸畑祇園大山笠]]は、当社および区内の[[菅原神社 (北九州市戸畑区)|菅原神社]]・中原八幡宮の祭りで、国の[[重要無形民俗文化財]]となっている。『[[筑前国続風土記]]』によれば、[[享和]]2年（[[1802年]]）、戸畑村内に疫病が蔓延し、飛幡八幡宮の[[須佐之男命]]に平癒を祈願した所終息したため、翌享和3年（[[1803年]]）から始められたものである。 == ギャラリー == {{gallery |ファイル:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_01.jpg|一の鳥居 |ファイル:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_03.jpg|正一位稲荷大明神 }} == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == * [https://tdetode.jimdofree.com/ 飛幡八幡宮] - 公式サイト {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とひはたはちまんくう}} [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:福岡県の旧県社]] [[Category:北九州市の神社]] [[Category:戸畑区の歴史]] [[Category:戸畑区の建築物]] [[Category:八幡宮]]

It was formerly ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} and is currently listed as a {{ill|Special Shrine|ja|別表神社}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}} ({{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} and {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}), {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}}, and {{ill|Dōsojin|ja|道祖神}}. == History == In the Kenkyū period (1190-1199), {{ill|Utsunomiya Shigeie|ja|宇都宮重業}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏}} ({{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏}}), brought the Hachiman deity from his hometown of Utsunomiya and enshrined it in Miyata Mountain in Edamitsu Village, which was the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門}} (northeast) of {{ill|Hanao Castle|ja|花尾城}}. Later, the shrine was moved to Tobata Village and became the guardian deity of Tobata and Nakahara Villages. During the Edo period, it was designated a prayer site by the {{ill|Kuroda clan|ja|黒田氏}}. In 1920, it was relocated to its current site in Asano. In 1931, it was designated a Prefectural Shrine. In 1995, the shrine's name was changed from "Tobata Hachiman Shrine" to "Tobihata Hachimangu." == festivals == The {{ill|Tobata Gion Yamakasa|ja|戸畑祇園大山笠}} festival, held on the fourth weekend of July, is a major event at the shrine and at {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu)|ja|菅原神社 (北九州市戸畑区)}} and Nakahara Hachiman Shrine in the ward. It is designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} of Japan. According to the {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記}}, the festival began in Kyōwa 2 (1802) when an epidemic broke out in Tobata Village. After praying for healing at the shrine to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}}, the epidemic ceased, and the festival was started the following year. == Gallery == {{gallery |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_01.jpg|First Torii |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_03.jpg|Shoichii Inari Daimyojin }} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tobihata Hachimangu}} [[Category:Beppyo Shrines]] [[Category:Prefectural Shrines of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:History of Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:[[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Original pages]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]][[category:original pages]][[fr:User:Immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:Pages from wikipedia]] [[Category:Pages_from_Simple_English_Wikipedia]][[Category:Pages_from_Simple_English_Wikipedia]] ]]

[[sv:Tobihata-hachimangū]] [[Category:dd]][[ja:飛幡八幡宮]] {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Tobihata Hachimangu |image=[[File:Tobihata Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_02.jpg|300px]] |location= 2-2-2 Asano, Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu, Fukuoka Prefecture |position= {{Coord|33|53|36|N|130|49|38|E|region:JP-40_scale:30000|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}
{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}}
{{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神}}
{{ill|Dōsojin|ja|道祖神}} |rank= Prefectural Shrine, Special Shrine |founded= Kenkyū period (1190-1199) |festival= October 14 |rituals= Tobata Gion Yamakasa Event
Hōjōe }} '''Tobihata Hachimangu''' (飛幡八幡宮[[Category:dd]][[ja:飛幡八幡宮]]) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu|ja|戸畑区}}, {{ill|Kitakyushu|ja|北九州市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}. It was formerly ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} and is currently listed as a {{ill|Special Shrine|ja|別表神社}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}} ({{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} and {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}), {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}}, and {{ill|Dōsojin|ja|道祖神}}. == History == In the Kenkyū period (1190-1199), {{ill|Utsunomiya Shigeie|ja|宇都宮重業}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏}} ({{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏}}), brought the Hachiman deity from his hometown of Utsunomiya and enshrined it in Miyata Mountain in Edamitsu Village, which was the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門}} (northeast) of {{ill|Hanao Castle|ja|花尾城}}. Later, the shrine was moved to Tobata Village and became the guardian deity of Tobata and Nakahara Villages. During the Edo period, it was designated a prayer site by the {{ill|Kuroda clan|ja|黒田氏}}. In 1920, it was relocated to its current site in Asano. In 1931, it was designated a Prefectural Shrine. In 1995, the shrine's name was changed from "Tobata Hachiman Shrine" to "Tobihata Hachimangu." == festivals == The {{ill|Tobata Gion Yamakasa|ja|戸畑祇園大山笠}} festival, held on the fourth weekend of July, is a major event at the shrine and at {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu)|ja|菅原神社 (北九州市戸畑区)}} and Nakahara Hachiman Shrine in the ward. It is designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} of Japan. According to the {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記}}, the festival began in Kyōwa 2 (1802) when an epidemic broke out in Tobata Village. After praying for healing at the shrine to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}}, the epidemic ceased, and the festival was started the following year. == Gallery == {{gallery |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_01.jpg|First Torii |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_03.jpg|Shoichii Inari Daimyojin }} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tobihata Hachimangu}} [[Category:Prefectural Shrines of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:History of Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:[[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]][[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]][[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Tobihata Hachimangu]][[Category:dd]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]]]]

Hōjōe }} '''Tobihata Hachimangu''' (飛幡八幡宮[[Category:dd]][[ja:飛幡八幡宮]]) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu|ja|戸畑区}}, {{ill|Kitakyushu|ja|北九州市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}. It was formerly ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} and is currently listed as a {{ill|Special Shrine|ja|別表神社}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}} ({{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} and {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}), {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}}, and {{ill|Dōsojin|ja|道祖神}}. == History == In the Kenkyū period (1190-1199), {{ill|Utsunomiya Shigeie|ja|宇都宮重業}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏}} ({{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏}}), brought the Hachiman deity from his hometown of Utsunomiya and enshrined it in Miyata Mountain in Edamitsu Village, which was the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門}} (northeast) of {{ill|Hanao Castle|ja|花尾城}}. Later, the shrine was moved to Tobata Village and became the guardian deity of Tobata and Nakahara Villages. During the Edo period, it was designated a prayer site by the {{ill|Kuroda clan|ja|黒田氏}}. In 1920, it was relocated to its current site in Asano. In 1931, it was designated a Prefectural Shrine. In 1995, the shrine's name was changed from "Tobata Hachiman Shrine" to "Tobihata Hachimangu." == festivals == The {{ill|Tobata Gion Yamakasa|ja|戸畑祇園大山笠}} festival, held on the fourth weekend of July, is a major event at the shrine and at {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu)|ja|菅原神社 (北九州市戸畑区)}} and Nakahara Hachiman Shrine in the ward. It is designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} of Japan. According to the {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記}}, the festival began in Kyōwa 2 (1802) when an epidemic broke out in Tobata Village. After praying for healing at the shrine to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}}, the epidemic ceased, and the festival was started the following year. == Gallery == {{gallery |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_01.jpg|First Torii |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_03.jpg|Shoichii Inari Daimyojin }} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tobihata Hachimangu}} [[Category:Prefectural Shrines of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:History of Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:[[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]][[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]][[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Tobihata Hachimangu]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]]]]

{{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神}}
{{ill|Dōsojin|ja|道祖神}} |rank= Prefectural Shrine, Special Shrine |founded= Kenkyū period (1190-1199) |festival= October 14 |rituals= Tobata Gion Yamakasa Event
Hōjōe }} '''Tobihata Hachimangu''' (飛幡八幡宮[[Category:dd]][[ja:飛幡八幡宮]]) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu|ja|戸畑区}}, {{ill|Kitakyushu|ja|北九州市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}. It was formerly ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} and is currently listed as a {{ill|Special Shrine|ja|別表神社}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}} ({{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} and {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}), {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}}, and {{ill|Dōsojin|ja|道祖神}}. == History == In the Kenkyū period (1190-1199), {{ill|Utsunomiya Shigeie|ja|宇都宮重業}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏}} ({{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏}}), brought the Hachiman deity from his hometown of Utsunomiya and enshrined it in Miyata Mountain in Edamitsu Village, which was the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門}} (northeast) of {{ill|Hanao Castle|ja|花尾城}}. Later, the shrine was moved to Tobata Village and became the guardian deity of Tobata and Nakahara Villages. During the Edo period, it was designated a prayer site by the {{ill|Kuroda clan|ja|黒田氏}}. In 1920, it was relocated to its current site in Asano. In 1931, it was designated a Prefectural Shrine. In 1995, the shrine's name was changed from "Tobata Hachiman Shrine" to "Tobihata Hachimangu." == festivals == The {{ill|Tobata Gion Yamakasa|ja|戸畑祇園大山笠}} festival, held on the fourth weekend of July, is a major event at the shrine and at {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu)|ja|菅原神社 (北九州市戸畑区)}} and Nakahara Hachiman Shrine in the ward. It is designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} of Japan. According to the {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記}}, the festival began in Kyōwa 2 (1802) when an epidemic broke out in Tobata Village. After praying for healing at the shrine to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}}, the epidemic ceased, and the festival was started the following year. == Gallery == {{gallery |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_01.jpg|First Torii |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_03.jpg|Shoichii Inari Daimyojin }} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tobihata Hachimangu}} [[Category:Prefectural Shrines of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:History of Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Tobihata Hachimangu]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]]]]

It was formerly ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} and is currently listed as a {{ill|Special Shrine|ja|別表神社}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}} ({{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} and {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}), {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}}, and {{ill|Dōsojin|ja|道祖神}}. == History == In the Kenkyū period (1190-1199), {{ill|Utsunomiya Shigeie|ja|宇都宮重業}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏}} ({{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏}}), brought the Hachiman deity from his hometown of Utsunomiya and enshrined it in Miyata Mountain in Edamitsu Village, which was the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門}} (northeast) of {{ill|Hanao Castle|ja|花尾城}}. Later, the shrine was moved to Tobata Village and became the guardian deity of Tobata and Nakahara Villages. During the Edo period, it was designated a prayer site by the {{ill|Kuroda clan|ja|黒田氏}}. In 1920, it was relocated to its current site in Asano. In 1931, it was designated a Prefectural Shrine. In 1995, the shrine's name was changed from "Tobata Hachiman Shrine" to "Tobihata Hachimangu." == festivals == The {{ill|Tobata Gion Yamakasa|ja|戸畑祇園大山笠}} festival, held on the fourth weekend of July, is a major event at the shrine and at {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu)|ja|菅原神社 (北九州市戸畑区)}} and Nakahara Hachiman Shrine in the ward. It is designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} of Japan. According to the {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記}}, the festival began in Kyōwa 2 (1802) when an epidemic broke out in Tobata Village. After praying for healing at the shrine to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}}, the epidemic ceased, and the festival was started the following year. == Gallery == {{gallery |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_01.jpg|First Torii |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_03.jpg|Shoichii Inari Daimyojin }} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tobihata Hachimangu}} [[Category:Prefectural Shrines of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:History of Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category: [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Tobihata Hachimangu]][[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]]]]

Later, the shrine was moved to Tobata Village and became the guardian deity of Tobata and Nakahara Villages. During the Edo period, it was designated a prayer site by the {{ill|Kuroda clan|ja|黒田氏}}. In 1920, it was relocated to its current site in Asano. In 1931, it was designated a Prefectural Shrine. In 1995, the shrine's name was changed from "Tobata Hachiman Shrine" to "Tobihata Hachimangu." == festivals == The {{ill|Tobata Gion Yamakasa|ja|戸畑祇園大山笠}} festival, held on the fourth weekend of July, is a major event at the shrine and at {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu)|ja|菅原神社 (北九州市戸畑区)}} and Nakahara Hachiman Shrine in the ward. It is designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} of Japan. According to the {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記}}, the festival began in Kyōwa 2 (1802) when an epidemic broke out in Tobata Village. After praying for healing at the shrine to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}}, the epidemic ceased, and the festival was started the following year. == Gallery == {{gallery |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_01.jpg|First Torii |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_03.jpg|Shoichii Inari Daimyojin }} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tobihata Hachimangu}} [[Category:Prefectural Shrines of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:History of Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category: [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Tobihata Hachimangu]][[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-27]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]]]]

It was formerly ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} and is currently listed as a {{ill|Special Shrine|ja|別表神社}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}} ({{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} and {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}), {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}}, and {{ill|Dōsojin|ja|道祖神}}. == History == In the Kenkyū period (1190-1199), {{ill|Utsunomiya Shigeie|ja|宇都宮重業}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏}} ({{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏}}), brought the Hachiman deity from his hometown of Utsunomiya and enshrined it in Miyata Mountain in Edamitsu Village, which was the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門}} (northeast) of {{ill|Hanao Castle|ja|花尾城}}. Later, the shrine was moved to Tobata Village and became the guardian deity of Tobata and Nakahara Villages. During the Edo period, it was designated a prayer site by the {{ill|Kuroda clan|ja|黒田氏}}. In 1920, it was relocated to its current site in Asano. In 1931, it was designated a Prefectural Shrine. In 1995, the shrine's name was changed from "Tobata Hachiman Shrine" to "Tobihata Hachimangu." == festivals == The {{ill|Tobata Gion Yamakasa|ja|戸畑祇園大山笠}} festival, held on the fourth weekend of July, is a major event at the shrine and at {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu)|ja|菅原神社 (北九州市戸畑区)}} and Nakahara Hachiman Shrine in the ward. It is designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} of Japan. According to the {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記}}, the festival began in Kyōwa 2 (1802) when an epidemic broke out in Tobata Village. After praying for healing at the shrine to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}}, the epidemic ceased, and the festival was started the following year. == Gallery == {{gallery |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_01.jpg|First Torii |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_03.jpg|Shoichii Inari Daimyojin }} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tobihata Hachimangu}} [[Category:Prefectural Shrines of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:History of Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category: [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Tobihata Hachimangu]][[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-27]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Tobihata Hachimangu]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tobihata Hachimangu]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]][[en:Tobihata Hachimangu]][[Category:qq]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]]]]

[[sv:Tobihata-hachimangū]] [[ja:飛幡八幡宮]] {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Tobihata Hachimangu |image=[[File:Tobihata Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_02.jpg|300px]] |location= 2-2-2 Asano, Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu, Fukuoka Prefecture |position= {{Coord|33|53|36|N|130|49|38|E|region:JP-40_scale:30000|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}
{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}}
{{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神}}
{{ill|Dōsojin|ja|道祖神}} |rank= Prefectural Shrine, Special Shrine |founded= Kenkyū period (1190-1199) |festival= October 14 |rituals= Tobata Gion Yamakasa Event
Hōjōe }} '''Tobihata Hachimangu''' (飛幡八幡宮[[ja:飛幡八幡宮]]) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu|ja|戸畑区}}, {{ill|Kitakyushu|ja|北九州市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}. It was formerly ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} and is currently listed as a {{ill|Special Shrine|ja|別表神社}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}} ({{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} and {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}), {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}}, and {{ill|Dōsojin|ja|道祖神}}. == History == In the Kenkyū period (1190-1199), {{ill|Utsunomiya Shigeie|ja|宇都宮重業}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏}} ({{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏}}), brought the Hachiman deity from his hometown of Utsunomiya and enshrined it in Miyata Mountain in Edamitsu Village, which was the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門}} (northeast) of {{ill|Hanao Castle|ja|花尾城}}. Later, the shrine was moved to Tobata Village and became the guardian deity of Tobata and Nakahara Villages. During the Edo period, it was designated a prayer site by the {{ill|Kuroda clan|ja|黒田氏}}. In 1920, it was relocated to its current site in Asano. In 1931, it was designated a Prefectural Shrine. In 1995, the shrine's name was changed from "Tobata Hachiman Shrine" to "Tobihata Hachimangu." == festivals == The {{ill|Tobata Gion Yamakasa|ja|戸畑祇園大山笠}} festival, held on the fourth weekend of July, is a major event at the shrine and at {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu)|ja|菅原神社 (北九州市戸畑区)}} and Nakahara Hachiman Shrine in the ward. It is designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} of Japan. According to the {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記}}, the festival began in Kyōwa 2 (1802) when an epidemic broke out in Tobata Village. After praying for healing at the shrine to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}}, the epidemic ceased, and the festival was started the following year. == Gallery == {{gallery |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_01.jpg|First Torii |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_03.jpg|Shoichii Inari Daimyojin }} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tobihata Hachimangu}} [[Category:Prefectural Shrines of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:History of Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}
{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}}
{{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神}}
{{ill|Dōsojin|ja|道祖神}} |rank= Prefectural Shrine, Special Shrine |founded= Kenkyū period (1190-1199) |festival= October 14 |rituals= Tobata Gion Yamakasa Event
Hōjōe }} '''Tobihata Hachimangu''' (飛幡八幡宮[[ja:飛幡八幡宮]]) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu|ja|戸畑区}}, {{ill|Kitakyushu|ja|北九州市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}. It was formerly ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} and is currently listed as a {{ill|Special Shrine|ja|別表神社}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}} ({{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} and {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}), {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}}, and {{ill|Dōsojin|ja|道祖神}}. == History == In the Kenkyū period (1190-1199), {{ill|Utsunomiya Shigeie|ja|宇都宮重業}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏}} ({{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏}}), brought the Hachiman deity from his hometown of Utsunomiya and enshrined it in Miyata Mountain in Edamitsu Village, which was the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門}} (northeast) of {{ill|Hanao Castle|ja|花尾城}}. Later, the shrine was moved to Tobata Village and became the guardian deity of Tobata and Nakahara Villages. During the Edo period, it was designated a prayer site by the {{ill|Kuroda clan|ja|黒田氏}}. In 1920, it was relocated to its current site in Asano. In 1931, it was designated a Prefectural Shrine. In 1995, the shrine's name was changed from "Tobata Hachiman Shrine" to "Tobihata Hachimangu." == festivals == The {{ill|Tobata Gion Yamakasa|ja|戸畑祇園大山笠}} festival, held on the fourth weekend of July, is a major event at the shrine and at {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu)|ja|菅原神社 (北九州市戸畑区)}} and Nakahara Hachiman Shrine in the ward. It is designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} of Japan. According to the {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記}}, the festival began in Kyōwa 2 (1802) when an epidemic broke out in Tobata Village. After praying for healing at the shrine to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}}, the epidemic ceased, and the festival was started the following year. == Gallery == {{gallery Hōjōe }} '''Tobihata Hachimangu''' (飛幡八幡宮[[ja:飛幡八幡宮]]) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu|ja|戸畑区}}, {{ill|Kitakyushu|ja|北九州市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}. It was formerly ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} and is currently listed as a {{ill|Special Shrine|ja|別表神社}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}} ({{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} and {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}), {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}}, and {{ill|Dōsojin|ja|道祖神}}. == History == In the Kenkyū period (1190-1199), {{ill|Utsunomiya Shigeie|ja|宇都宮重業}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏}} ({{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏}}), brought the Hachiman deity from his hometown of Utsunomiya and enshrined it in Miyata Mountain in Edamitsu Village, which was the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門}} (northeast) of {{ill|Hanao Castle|ja|花尾城}}. Later, the shrine was moved to Tobata Village and became the guardian deity of Tobata and Nakahara Villages. During the Edo period, it was designated a prayer site by the {{ill|Kuroda clan|ja|黒田氏}}. In 1920, it was relocated to its current site in Asano. In 1931, it was designated a Prefectural Shrine. In 1995, the shrine's name was changed from "Tobata Hachiman Shrine" to "Tobihata Hachimangu." == festivals == The {{ill|Tobata Gion Yamakasa|ja|戸畑祇園大山笠}} festival, held on the fourth weekend of July, is a major event at the shrine and at {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu)|ja|菅原神社 (北九州市戸畑区)}} and Nakahara Hachiman Shrine in the ward. It is designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} of Japan. According to the {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記}}, the festival began in Kyōwa 2 (1802) when an epidemic broke out in Tobata Village. After praying for healing at the shrine to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}}, the epidemic ceased, and the festival was started the following year. == Gallery == {{gallery [[sv:Tobihata-hachimangū]] [[ja:飛幡八幡宮]] {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Tobihata Hachimangu |image=[[File:Tobihata Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_02.jpg|300px]] |location= 2-2-2 Asano, Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu, Fukuoka Prefecture |position= {{Coord|33|53|36|N|130|49|38|E|region:JP-40_scale:30000|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}
{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}}
{{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神}}
{{ill|Dōsojin|ja|道祖神}} |rank= Prefectural Shrine, Special Shrine |founded= Kenkyū period (1190-1199) |festival= October 14 |rituals= Tobata Gion Yamakasa Event
Hōjōe }} '''Tobihata Hachimangu''' (飛幡八幡宮[[ja:飛幡八幡宮]]) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu|ja|戸畑区}}, {{ill|Kitakyushu|ja|北九州市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}. It was formerly ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} and is currently listed as a {{ill|Special Shrine|ja|別表神社}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}} ({{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} and {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}), {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}}, and {{ill|Dōsojin|ja|道祖神}}. == History == In the Kenkyū period (1190-1199), {{ill|Utsunomiya Shigeie|ja|宇都宮重業}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏}} ({{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏}}), brought the Hachiman deity from his hometown of Utsunomiya and enshrined it in Miyata Mountain in Edamitsu Village, which was the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門}} (northeast) of {{ill|Hanao Castle|ja|花尾城}}. Later, the shrine was moved to Tobata Village and became the guardian deity of Tobata and Nakahara Villages. During the Edo period, it was designated a prayer site by the {{ill|Kuroda clan|ja|黒田氏}}. In 1920, it was relocated to its current site in Asano. In 1931, it was designated a Prefectural Shrine. In 1995, the shrine's name was changed from "Tobata Hachiman Shrine" to "Tobihata Hachimangu." == festivals == The {{ill|Tobata Gion Yamakasa|ja|戸畑祇園大山笠}} festival, held on the fourth weekend of July, is a major event at the shrine and at {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu)|ja|菅原神社 (北九州市戸畑区)}} and Nakahara Hachiman Shrine in the ward. It is designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} of Japan. According to the {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記}}, the festival began in Kyōwa 2 (1802) when an epidemic broke out in Tobata Village. After praying for healing at the shrine to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}}, the epidemic ceased, and the festival was started the following year. == Gallery == {{gallery |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_01.jpg|First Torii |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_03.jpg|Shoichii Inari Daimyojin }} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tobihata It is designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} of Japan. According to the {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記}}, the festival began in Kyōwa 2 (1802) when an epidemic broke out in Tobata Village. After praying for healing at the shrine to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}}, the epidemic ceased, and the festival was started the following year. == Gallery == {{gallery [[sv:Tobihata-hachimangū]] [[ja:飛幡八幡宮]] {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Tobihata Hachimangu |image=[[File:Tobihata Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_02.jpg|300px]] |location= 2-2-2 Asano, Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu, Fukuoka Prefecture |position= {{Coord|33|53|36|N|130|49|38|E|region:JP-40_scale:30000|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}
{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}}
{{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神}}
{{ill|Dōsojin|ja|道祖神}} |rank= Prefectural Shrine, Special Shrine |founded= Kenkyū period (1190-1199) |festival= October 14 |rituals= Tobata Gion Yamakasa Event
Hōjōe }} '''Tobihata Hachimangu''' (飛幡八幡宮[[ja:飛幡八幡宮]]) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu|ja|戸畑区}}, {{ill|Kitakyushu|ja|北九州市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}. It was formerly ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} and is currently listed as a {{ill|Special Shrine|ja|別表神社}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}} ({{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} and {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}), {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}}, and {{ill|Dōsojin|ja|道祖神}}. == History == In the Kenkyū period (1190-1199), {{ill|Utsunomiya Shigeie|ja|宇都宮重業}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏}} ({{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏}}), brought the Hachiman deity from his hometown of Utsunomiya and enshrined it in Miyata Mountain in Edamitsu Village, which was the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門}} (northeast) of {{ill|Hanao Castle|ja|花尾城}}. Later, the shrine was moved to Tobata Village and became the guardian deity of Tobata and Nakahara Villages. During the Edo period, it was designated a prayer site by the {{ill|Kuroda clan|ja|黒田氏}}. In 1920, it was relocated to its current site in Asano. In 1931, it was designated a Prefectural Shrine. In 1995, the shrine's name was changed from "Tobata Hachiman Shrine" to "Tobihata Hachimangu." == festivals == The {{ill|Tobata Gion Yamakasa|ja|戸畑祇園大山笠}} festival, held on the fourth weekend of July, is a major event at the shrine and at {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu)|ja|菅原神社 (北九州市戸畑区)}} and Nakahara Hachiman Shrine in the ward. It is designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} of Japan. According to the {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記}}, the festival began in Kyōwa 2 (1802) when an epidemic broke out in Tobata Village. After praying for healing at the shrine to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}}, the epidemic ceased, and the festival was started the following year. == Gallery == {{gallery |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_01.jpg|First Torii |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_03.jpg|Shoichii Inari Daimyojin }} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tobihata [[sv:Tobihata-hachimangū]] [[ja:飛幡八幡宮]] {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Tobihata Hachimangu |image=[[File:Tobihata Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_02.jpg|300px]] |location= 2-2-2 Asano, Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu, Fukuoka Prefecture |position= {{Coord|33|53|36|N|130|49|38|E|region:JP-40_scale:30000|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}
{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}}
{{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神}}
{{ill|Dōsojin|ja|道祖神}} |rank= Prefectural Shrine, Special Shrine |founded= Kenkyū period (1190-1199) |festival= October 14 |rituals= Tobata Gion Yamakasa Event
Hōjōe }} '''Tobihata Hachimangu''' (飛幡八幡宮[[ja:飛幡八幡宮]]) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu|ja|戸畑区}}, {{ill|Kitakyushu|ja|北九州市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}. It was formerly ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} and is currently listed as a {{ill|Special Shrine|ja|別表神社}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}} ({{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} and {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}), {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}}, and {{ill|Dōsojin|ja|道祖神}}. == History == In the Kenkyū period (1190-1199), {{ill|Utsunomiya Shigeie|ja|宇都宮重業}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏}} ({{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏}}), brought the Hachiman deity from his hometown of Utsunomiya and enshrined it in Miyata Mountain in Edamitsu Village, which was the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門}} (northeast) of {{ill|Hanao Castle|ja|花尾城}}. Later, the shrine was moved to Tobata Village and became the guardian deity of Tobata and Nakahara Villages. During the Edo period, it was designated a prayer site by the {{ill|Kuroda clan|ja|黒田氏}}. In 1920, it was relocated to its current site in Asano. In 1931, it was designated a Prefectural Shrine. In 1995, the shrine's name was changed from "Tobata Hachiman Shrine" to "Tobihata Hachimangu." == festivals == The {{ill|Tobata Gion Yamakasa|ja|戸畑祇園大山笠}} festival, held on the fourth weekend of July, is a major event at the shrine and at {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu)|ja|菅原神社 (北九州市戸畑区)}} and Nakahara Hachiman Shrine in the ward. It is designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} of Japan. According to the {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記}}, the festival began in Kyōwa 2 (1802) when an epidemic broke out in Tobata Village. After praying for healing at the shrine to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}}, the epidemic ceased, and the festival was started the following year. == Gallery == {{gallery |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_01.jpg|First Torii |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_03.jpg|Shoichii Inari Daimyojin }} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tobihata Later, the shrine was moved to Tobata Village and became the guardian deity of Tobata and Nakahara Villages. During the Edo period, it was designated a prayer site by the {{ill|Kuroda clan|ja|黒田氏}}. In 1920, it was relocated to its current site in Asano. In 1931, it was designated a Prefectural Shrine. In 1995, the shrine's name was changed from "Tobata Hachiman Shrine" to "Tobihata Hachimangu." == festivals == The {{ill|Tobata Gion Yamakasa|ja|戸畑祇園大山笠}} festival, held on the fourth weekend of July, is a major event at the shrine and at {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu)|ja|菅原神社 (北九州市戸畑区)}} and Nakahara Hachiman Shrine in the ward. It is designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} of Japan. According to the {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記}}, the festival began in Kyōwa 2 (1802) when an epidemic broke out in Tobata Village. After praying for healing at the shrine to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}}, the epidemic ceased, and the festival was started the following year. == Gallery == {{gallery |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_01.jpg|First Torii |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_03.jpg|Shoichii Inari Daimyojin }} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tobihata [[sv:Tobihata-hachimangū]] [[ja:飛幡八幡宮]] {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Tobihata Hachimangu |image=[[File:Tobihata Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_02.jpg|300px]] |location= 2-2-2 Asano, Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu, Fukuoka Prefecture |position= {{Coord|33|53|36|N|130|49|38|E|region:JP-40_scale:30000|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}
{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}}
{{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神}}
{{ill|Dōsojin|ja|道祖神}} |rank= Prefectural Shrine, Special Shrine |founded= Kenkyū period (1190-1199) |festival= October 14 |rituals= Tobata Gion Yamakasa Event
Hōjōe }} '''Tobihata Hachimangu''' (飛幡八幡宮[[ja:飛幡八幡宮]]) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu|ja|戸畑区}}, {{ill|Kitakyushu|ja|北九州市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}. It was formerly ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} and is currently listed as a {{ill|Special Shrine|ja|別表神社}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}} ({{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} and {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}), {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}}, and {{ill|Dōsojin|ja|道祖神}}. == History == In the Kenkyū period (1190-1199), {{ill|Utsunomiya Shigeie|ja|宇都宮重業}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏}} ({{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏}}), brought the Hachiman deity from his hometown of Utsunomiya and enshrined it in Miyata Mountain in Edamitsu Village, which was the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門}} (northeast) of {{ill|Hanao Castle|ja|花尾城}}. Later, the shrine was moved to Tobata Village and became the guardian deity of Tobata and Nakahara Villages. During the Edo period, it was designated a prayer site by the {{ill|Kuroda clan|ja|黒田氏}}. In 1920, it was relocated to its current site in Asano. In 1931, it was designated a Prefectural Shrine. In 1995, the shrine's name was changed from "Tobata Hachiman Shrine" to "Tobihata Hachimangu." == festivals == The {{ill|Tobata Gion Yamakasa|ja|戸畑祇園大山笠}} festival, held on the fourth weekend of July, is a major event at the shrine and at {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu)|ja|菅原神社 (北九州市戸畑区)}} and Nakahara Hachiman Shrine in the ward. It is designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} of Japan. According to the {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記}}, the festival began in Kyōwa 2 (1802) when an epidemic broke out in Tobata Village. After praying for healing at the shrine to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}}, the epidemic ceased, and the festival was started the following year. == Gallery == {{gallery |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_01.jpg|First Torii |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_03.jpg|Shoichii Inari Daimyojin }} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tobihata Hachimangu}} [[Category:Prefectural Shrines of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:History of Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in It was formerly ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} and is currently listed as a {{ill|Special Shrine|ja|別表神社}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}} ({{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} and {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}), {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}}, and {{ill|Dōsojin|ja|道祖神}}. == History == In the Kenkyū period (1190-1199), {{ill|Utsunomiya Shigeie|ja|宇都宮重業}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏}} ({{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏}}), brought the Hachiman deity from his hometown of Utsunomiya and enshrined it in Miyata Mountain in Edamitsu Village, which was the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門}} (northeast) of {{ill|Hanao Castle|ja|花尾城}}. Later, the shrine was moved to Tobata Village and became the guardian deity of Tobata and Nakahara Villages. During the Edo period, it was designated a prayer site by the {{ill|Kuroda clan|ja|黒田氏}}. In 1920, it was relocated to its current site in Asano. In 1931, it was designated a Prefectural Shrine. In 1995, the shrine's name was changed from "Tobata Hachiman Shrine" to "Tobihata Hachimangu." == festivals == The {{ill|Tobata Gion Yamakasa|ja|戸畑祇園大山笠}} festival, held on the fourth weekend of July, is a major event at the shrine and at {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu)|ja|菅原神社 (北九州市戸畑区)}} and Nakahara Hachiman Shrine in the ward. It is designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} of Japan. According to the {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記}}, the festival began in Kyōwa 2 (1802) when an epidemic broke out in Tobata Village. After praying for healing at the shrine to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}}, the epidemic ceased, and the festival was started the following year. == Gallery == {{gallery [[sv:Tobihata-hachimangū]] [[ja:飛幡八幡宮]] {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Tobihata Hachimangu |image=[[File:Tobihata Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_02.jpg|300px]] |location= 2-2-2 Asano, Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu, Fukuoka Prefecture |position= {{Coord|33|53|36|N|130|49|38|E|region:JP-40_scale:30000|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}
{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}}
{{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神}}
{{ill|Dōsojin|ja|道祖神}} |rank= Prefectural Shrine, Special Shrine |founded= Kenkyū period (1190-1199) |festival= October 14 |rituals= Tobata Gion Yamakasa Event
Hōjōe }} '''Tobihata Hachimangu''' (飛幡八幡宮[[ja:飛幡八幡宮]]) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu|ja|戸畑区}}, {{ill|Kitakyushu|ja|北九州市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}. It was formerly ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} and is currently listed as a {{ill|Special Shrine|ja|別表神社}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}} ({{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} and {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}), {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}}, and {{ill|Dōsojin|ja|道祖神}}. == History == In the Kenkyū period (1190-1199), {{ill|Utsunomiya Shigeie|ja|宇都宮重業}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏}} ({{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏}}), brought the Hachiman deity from his hometown of Utsunomiya and enshrined it in Miyata Mountain in Edamitsu Village, which was the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門}} (northeast) of {{ill|Hanao Castle|ja|花尾城}}. Later, the shrine was moved to Tobata Village and became the guardian deity of Tobata and Nakahara Villages. During the Edo period, it was designated a prayer site by the {{ill|Kuroda clan|ja|黒田氏}}. In 1920, it was relocated to its current site in Asano. In 1931, it was designated a Prefectural Shrine. In 1995, the shrine's name was changed from "Tobata Hachiman Shrine" to "Tobihata Hachimangu." == festivals == The {{ill|Tobata Gion Yamakasa|ja|戸畑祇園大山笠}} festival, held on the fourth weekend of July, is a major event at the shrine and at {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu)|ja|菅原神社 (北九州市戸畑区)}} and Nakahara Hachiman Shrine in the ward. It is designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} of Japan. According to the {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記}}, the festival began in Kyōwa 2 (1802) when an epidemic broke out in Tobata Village. After praying for healing at the shrine to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}}, the epidemic ceased, and the festival was started the following year. == Gallery == {{gallery |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_01.jpg|First Torii |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_03.jpg|Shoichii Inari Daimyojin }} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tobihata [[sv:Tobihata-hachimangū]] [[ja:飛幡八幡宮]] {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Tobihata Hachimangu |image=[[File:Tobihata Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_02.jpg|300px]] |location= 2-2-2 Asano, Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu, Fukuoka Prefecture |position= {{Coord|33|53|36|N|130|49|38|E|region:JP-40_scale:30000|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}
{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}}
{{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神}}
{{ill|Dōsojin|ja|道祖神}} |rank= Prefectural Shrine, Special Shrine |founded= Kenkyū period (1190-1199) |festival= October 14 |rituals= Tobata Gion Yamakasa Event
Hōjōe }} '''Tobihata Hachimangu''' (飛幡八幡宮[[ja:飛幡八幡宮]]) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu|ja|戸畑区}}, {{ill|Kitakyushu|ja|北九州市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}. It was formerly ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} and is currently listed as a {{ill|Special Shrine|ja|別表神社}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}} ({{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} and {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}), {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}}, and {{ill|Dōsojin|ja|道祖神}}. == History == In the Kenkyū period (1190-1199), {{ill|Utsunomiya Shigeie|ja|宇都宮重業}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏}} ({{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏}}), brought the Hachiman deity from his hometown of Utsunomiya and enshrined it in Miyata Mountain in Edamitsu Village, which was the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門}} (northeast) of {{ill|Hanao Castle|ja|花尾城}}. Later, the shrine was moved to Tobata Village and became the guardian deity of Tobata and Nakahara Villages. During the Edo period, it was designated a prayer site by the {{ill|Kuroda clan|ja|黒田氏}}. In 1920, it was relocated to its current site in Asano. In 1931, it was designated a Prefectural Shrine. In 1995, the shrine's name was changed from "Tobata Hachiman Shrine" to "Tobihata Hachimangu." == festivals == The {{ill|Tobata Gion Yamakasa|ja|戸畑祇園大山笠}} festival, held on the fourth weekend of July, is a major event at the shrine and at {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu)|ja|菅原神社 (北九州市戸畑区)}} and Nakahara Hachiman Shrine in the ward. It is designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} of Japan. According to the {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記}}, the festival began in Kyōwa 2 (1802) when an epidemic broke out in Tobata Village. After praying for healing at the shrine to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}}, the epidemic ceased, and the festival was started the following year. == Gallery == {{gallery |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_01.jpg|First Torii |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_03.jpg|Shoichii Inari Daimyojin }} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tobihata It was formerly ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} and is currently listed as a {{ill|Special Shrine|ja|別表神社}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}} ({{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} and {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}), {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}}, and {{ill|Dōsojin|ja|道祖神}}. == History == In the Kenkyū period (1190-1199), {{ill|Utsunomiya Shigeie|ja|宇都宮重業}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏}} ({{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏}}), brought the Hachiman deity from his hometown of Utsunomiya and enshrined it in Miyata Mountain in Edamitsu Village, which was the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門}} (northeast) of {{ill|Hanao Castle|ja|花尾城}}. Later, the shrine was moved to Tobata Village and became the guardian deity of Tobata and Nakahara Villages. During the Edo period, it was designated a prayer site by the {{ill|Kuroda clan|ja|黒田氏}}. In 1920, it was relocated to its current site in Asano. In 1931, it was designated a Prefectural Shrine. In 1995, the shrine's name was changed from "Tobata Hachiman Shrine" to "Tobihata Hachimangu." == festivals == The {{ill|Tobata Gion Yamakasa|ja|戸畑祇園大山笠}} festival, held on the fourth weekend of July, is a major event at the shrine and at {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu)|ja|菅原神社 (北九州市戸畑区)}} and Nakahara Hachiman Shrine in the ward. It is designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} of Japan. According to the {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記}}, the festival began in Kyōwa 2 (1802) when an epidemic broke out in Tobata Village. After praying for healing at the shrine to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}}, the epidemic ceased, and the festival was started the following year. == Gallery == {{gallery [[sv:Tobihata-hachimangū]] [[ja:飛幡八幡宮]] {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Tobihata Hachimangu |image=[[File:Tobihata Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_02.jpg|300px]] |location= 2-2-2 Asano, Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu, Fukuoka Prefecture |position= {{Coord|33|53|36|N|130|49|38|E|region:JP-40_scale:30000|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}
{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}}
{{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神}}
{{ill|Dōsojin|ja|道祖神}} |rank= Prefectural Shrine, Special Shrine |founded= Kenkyū period (1190-1199) |festival= October 14 |rituals= Tobata Gion Yamakasa Event
Hōjōe }} '''Tobihata Hachimangu''' (飛幡八幡宮[[ja:飛幡八幡宮]]) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu|ja|戸畑区}}, {{ill|Kitakyushu|ja|北九州市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}. It was formerly ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} and is currently listed as a {{ill|Special Shrine|ja|別表神社}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}} ({{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} and {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}), {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}}, and {{ill|Dōsojin|ja|道祖神}}. == History == In the Kenkyū period (1190-1199), {{ill|Utsunomiya Shigeie|ja|宇都宮重業}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏}} ({{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏}}), brought the Hachiman deity from his hometown of Utsunomiya and enshrined it in Miyata Mountain in Edamitsu Village, which was the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門}} (northeast) of {{ill|Hanao Castle|ja|花尾城}}. Later, the shrine was moved to Tobata Village and became the guardian deity of Tobata and Nakahara Villages. During the Edo period, it was designated a prayer site by the {{ill|Kuroda clan|ja|黒田氏}}. In 1920, it was relocated to its current site in Asano. In 1931, it was designated a Prefectural Shrine. In 1995, the shrine's name was changed from "Tobata Hachiman Shrine" to "Tobihata Hachimangu." == festivals == The {{ill|Tobata Gion Yamakasa|ja|戸畑祇園大山笠}} festival, held on the fourth weekend of July, is a major event at the shrine and at {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu)|ja|菅原神社 (北九州市戸畑区)}} and Nakahara Hachiman Shrine in the ward. It is designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} of Japan. According to the {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記}}, the festival began in Kyōwa 2 (1802) when an epidemic broke out in Tobata Village. After praying for healing at the shrine to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}}, the epidemic ceased, and the festival was started the following year. == Gallery == {{gallery |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_01.jpg|First Torii |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_03.jpg|Shoichii Inari Daimyojin }} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tobihata Hachimangu}} [[Category:Prefectural Shrines of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:History of Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in [[sv:Tobihata-hachimangū]] [[ja:飛幡八幡宮]] {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Tobihata Hachimangu |image=[[File:Tobihata Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_02.jpg|300px]] |location= 2-2-2 Asano, Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu, Fukuoka Prefecture |position= {{Coord|33|53|36|N|130|49|38|E|region:JP-40_scale:30000|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}
{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}}
{{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神}}
{{ill|Dōsojin|ja|道祖神}} |rank= Prefectural Shrine, Special Shrine |founded= Kenkyū period (1190-1199) |festival= October 14 |rituals= Tobata Gion Yamakasa Event
Hōjōe }} '''Tobihata Hachimangu''' (飛幡八幡宮[[ja:飛幡八幡宮]]) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu|ja|戸畑区}}, {{ill|Kitakyushu|ja|北九州市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}. It was formerly ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} and is currently listed as a {{ill|Special Shrine|ja|別表神社}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}} ({{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} and {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}), {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}}, and {{ill|Dōsojin|ja|道祖神}}. == History == In the Kenkyū period (1190-1199), {{ill|Utsunomiya Shigeie|ja|宇都宮重業}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏}} ({{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏}}), brought the Hachiman deity from his hometown of Utsunomiya and enshrined it in Miyata Mountain in Edamitsu Village, which was the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門}} (northeast) of {{ill|Hanao Castle|ja|花尾城}}. Later, the shrine was moved to Tobata Village and became the guardian deity of Tobata and Nakahara Villages. During the Edo period, it was designated a prayer site by the {{ill|Kuroda clan|ja|黒田氏}}. In 1920, it was relocated to its current site in Asano. In 1931, it was designated a Prefectural Shrine. In 1995, the shrine's name was changed from "Tobata Hachiman Shrine" to "Tobihata Hachimangu." == festivals == The {{ill|Tobata Gion Yamakasa|ja|戸畑祇園大山笠}} festival, held on the fourth weekend of July, is a major event at the shrine and at {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu)|ja|菅原神社 (北九州市戸畑区)}} and Nakahara Hachiman Shrine in the ward. It is designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} of Japan. According to the {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記}}, the festival began in Kyōwa 2 (1802) when an epidemic broke out in Tobata Village. After praying for healing at the shrine to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}}, the epidemic ceased, and the festival was started the following year. == Gallery == {{gallery |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_01.jpg|First Torii |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_03.jpg|Shoichii Inari Daimyojin }} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tobihata It was formerly ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} and is currently listed as a {{ill|Special Shrine|ja|別表神社}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}} ({{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} and {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}), {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}}, and {{ill|Dōsojin|ja|道祖神}}. == History == In the Kenkyū period (1190-1199), {{ill|Utsunomiya Shigeie|ja|宇都宮重業}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏}} ({{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏}}), brought the Hachiman deity from his hometown of Utsunomiya and enshrined it in Miyata Mountain in Edamitsu Village, which was the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門}} (northeast) of {{ill|Hanao Castle|ja|花尾城}}. Later, the shrine was moved to Tobata Village and became the guardian deity of Tobata and Nakahara Villages. During the Edo period, it was designated a prayer site by the {{ill|Kuroda clan|ja|黒田氏}}. In 1920, it was relocated to its current site in Asano. In 1931, it was designated a Prefectural Shrine. In 1995, the shrine's name was changed from "Tobata Hachiman Shrine" to "Tobihata Hachimangu." == festivals == The {{ill|Tobata Gion Yamakasa|ja|戸畑祇園大山笠}} festival, held on the fourth weekend of July, is a major event at the shrine and at {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu)|ja|菅原神社 (北九州市戸畑区)}} and Nakahara Hachiman Shrine in the ward. It is designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} of Japan. According to the {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記}}, the festival began in Kyōwa 2 (1802) when an epidemic broke out in Tobata Village. After praying for healing at the shrine to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}}, the epidemic ceased, and the festival was started the following year. == Gallery == {{gallery [[sv:Tobihata-hachimangū]] [[ja:飛幡八幡宮]] {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Tobihata Hachimangu |image=[[File:Tobihata Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_02.jpg|300px]] |location= 2-2-2 Asano, Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu, Fukuoka Prefecture |position= {{Coord|33|53|36|N|130|49|38|E|region:JP-40_scale:30000|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}
{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}}
{{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神}}
{{ill|Dōsojin|ja|道祖神}} |rank= Prefectural Shrine, Special Shrine |founded= Kenkyū period (1190-1199) |festival= October 14 |rituals= Tobata Gion Yamakasa Event
Hōjōe }} '''Tobihata Hachimangu''' (飛幡八幡宮[[ja:飛幡八幡宮]]) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu|ja|戸畑区}}, {{ill|Kitakyushu|ja|北九州市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}. It was formerly ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} and is currently listed as a {{ill|Special Shrine|ja|別表神社}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}} ({{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} and {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}), {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}}, and {{ill|Dōsojin|ja|道祖神}}. == History == In the Kenkyū period (1190-1199), {{ill|Utsunomiya Shigeie|ja|宇都宮重業}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏}} ({{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏}}), brought the Hachiman deity from his hometown of Utsunomiya and enshrined it in Miyata Mountain in Edamitsu Village, which was the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門}} (northeast) of {{ill|Hanao Castle|ja|花尾城}}. Later, the shrine was moved to Tobata Village and became the guardian deity of Tobata and Nakahara Villages. During the Edo period, it was designated a prayer site by the {{ill|Kuroda clan|ja|黒田氏}}. In 1920, it was relocated to its current site in Asano. In 1931, it was designated a Prefectural Shrine. In 1995, the shrine's name was changed from "Tobata Hachiman Shrine" to "Tobihata Hachimangu." == festivals == The {{ill|Tobata Gion Yamakasa|ja|戸畑祇園大山笠}} festival, held on the fourth weekend of July, is a major event at the shrine and at {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu)|ja|菅原神社 (北九州市戸畑区)}} and Nakahara Hachiman Shrine in the ward. It is designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} of Japan. According to the {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記}}, the festival began in Kyōwa 2 (1802) when an epidemic broke out in Tobata Village. After praying for healing at the shrine to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}}, the epidemic ceased, and the festival was started the following year. == Gallery == {{gallery |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_01.jpg|First Torii |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_03.jpg|Shoichii Inari Daimyojin }} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tobihata Hachimangu}} [[Category:Prefectural Shrines of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:History of Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in
