[[sv:Tobihata-hachimangū]] [[ja:飛幡八幡宮]] {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Tobihata Hachimangu |image=[[File:Tobihata Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_02.jpg|300px]] |location= 2-2-2 Asano, Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu, Fukuoka Prefecture |position= {{Coord|33|53|36|N|130|49|38|E|region:JP-40_scale:30000|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}
{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}}
{{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神|lt=Hime-no-Ōkami|1=Himegami|3=比売神}}
{{ill|Dōsojin|ja|道祖神}} |rank= Prefectural Shrine, Special Shrine |founded= Kenkyū period (1190-1199) |festival= October 14 |rituals= Tobata Gion Yamakasa Event
Hōjōe }} '''Tobihata Hachimangu''' (飛幡八幡宮[[ja:飛幡八幡宮]]) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu|ja|戸畑区}}, {{ill|Kitakyushu|ja|北九州市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}. It was formerly ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} and is currently listed as a {{ill|Special Shrine|ja|別表神社}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}} ({{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} and {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}), {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神|lt=Hime-no-Ōkami|1=Himegami|3=比売神}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}}, and {{ill|Dōsojin|ja|道祖神}}. == History == In the Kenkyū period (1190-1199), {{ill|Utsunomiya Shigeie|ja|宇都宮重業}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏}} ({{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏}}), brought the Hachiman deity from his hometown of Utsunomiya and enshrined it in Miyata Mountain in Edamitsu Village, which was the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門}} (northeast) of {{ill|Hanao Castle|ja|花尾城}}. Later, the shrine was moved to Tobata Village and became the guardian deity of Tobata and Nakahara Villages. During the Edo period, it was designated a prayer site by the {{ill|Kuroda clan|ja|黒田氏}}. In 1920, it was relocated to its current site in Asano. In 1931, it was designated a Prefectural Shrine. In 1995, the shrine's name was changed from "Tobata Hachiman Shrine" to "Tobihata Hachimangu." == festivals == The {{ill|Tobata Gion Yamakasa|ja|戸畑祇園大山笠}} festival, held on the fourth weekend of July, is a major event at the shrine and at {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu)|ja|菅原神社 (北九州市戸畑区)}} and Nakahara Hachiman Shrine in the ward. It is designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} of Japan. According to the {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記}}, the festival began in Kyōwa 2 (1802) when an epidemic broke out in Tobata Village. After praying for healing at the shrine to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}}, the epidemic ceased, and the festival was started the following year. == Gallery == {{gallery |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_01.jpg|First Torii |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_03.jpg|Shoichii Inari Daimyojin }} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tobihata [[sv:Tobihata-hachimangū]] [[ja:飛幡八幡宮]] {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Tobihata Hachimangu |image=[[File:Tobihata Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_02.jpg|300px]] |location= 2-2-2 Asano, Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu, Fukuoka Prefecture |position= {{Coord|33|53|36|N|130|49|38|E|region:JP-40_scale:30000|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}
{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}}
{{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神|lt=Hime-no-Ōkami|1=Himegami|3=比売神}}
{{ill|Dōsojin|ja|道祖神}} |rank= Prefectural Shrine, Special Shrine |founded= Kenkyū period (1190-1199) |festival= October 14 |rituals= Tobata Gion Yamakasa Event
Hōjōe }} '''Tobihata Hachimangu''' (飛幡八幡宮[[ja:飛幡八幡宮]]) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu|ja|戸畑区}}, {{ill|Kitakyushu|ja|北九州市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}. It was formerly ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} and is currently listed as a {{ill|Special Shrine|ja|別表神社}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}} ({{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} and {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}), {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神|lt=Hime-no-Ōkami|1=Himegami|3=比売神}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}}, and {{ill|Dōsojin|ja|道祖神}}. == History == In the Kenkyū period (1190-1199), {{ill|Utsunomiya Shigeie|ja|宇都宮重業}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏}} ({{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏}}), brought the Hachiman deity from his hometown of Utsunomiya and enshrined it in Miyata Mountain in Edamitsu Village, which was the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門}} (northeast) of {{ill|Hanao Castle|ja|花尾城}}. Later, the shrine was moved to Tobata Village and became the guardian deity of Tobata and Nakahara Villages. During the Edo period, it was designated a prayer site by the {{ill|Kuroda clan|ja|黒田氏}}. In 1920, it was relocated to its current site in Asano. In 1931, it was designated a Prefectural Shrine. In 1995, the shrine's name was changed from "Tobata Hachiman Shrine" to "Tobihata Hachimangu." == festivals == The {{ill|Tobata Gion Yamakasa|ja|戸畑祇園大山笠}} festival, held on the fourth weekend of July, is a major event at the shrine and at {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu)|ja|菅原神社 (北九州市戸畑区)}} and Nakahara Hachiman Shrine in the ward. It is designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} of Japan. According to the {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記}}, the festival began in Kyōwa 2 (1802) when an epidemic broke out in Tobata Village. After praying for healing at the shrine to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}}, the epidemic ceased, and the festival was started the following year. == Gallery == {{gallery |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_01.jpg|First Torii |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_03.jpg|Shoichii Inari Daimyojin }} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tobihata {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Tobihata Hachimangu |image=[[File:Tobihata Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_02.jpg|300px]] |location= 2-2-2 Asano, Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu, Fukuoka Prefecture |position= {{Coord|33|53|36|N|130|49|38|E|region:JP-40_scale:30000|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft}}
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft}}
{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=draft}}
{{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神|lt=Hime-no-Ōkami|1=Himegami|3=比売神|12=draft}}
{{ill|Dōsojin|ja|道祖神|12=draft}} |rank= Prefectural Shrine, Special Shrine |founded= Kenkyū period (1190-1199) |festival= October 14 |rituals= Tobata Gion Yamakasa Event
Hōjōe }} '''Tobihata Hachimangu''' (飛幡八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu|ja|戸畑区|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kitakyushu|ja|北九州市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県|12=draft}}. It was formerly ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|12=draft}} and is currently listed as a {{ill|Special Shrine|ja|別表神社|12=draft}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|12=draft}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|12=draft}} ({{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft}} and {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft}}), {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神|lt=Hime-no-Ōkami|1=Himegami|3=比売神|12=draft}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Dōsojin|ja|道祖神|12=draft}}. == History == In the Kenkyū period (1190-1199), {{ill|Utsunomiya Shigeie|ja|宇都宮重業|12=draft}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏|12=draft}} ({{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏|12=draft}}), brought the Hachiman deity from his hometown of Utsunomiya and enshrined it in Miyata Mountain in Edamitsu Village, which was the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門|12=draft}} (northeast) of {{ill|Hanao Castle|ja|花尾城|12=draft}}. Later, the shrine was moved to Tobata Village and became the guardian deity of Tobata and Nakahara Villages. During the Edo period, it was designated a prayer site by the {{ill|Kuroda clan|ja|黒田氏|12=draft}}. In 1920, it was relocated to its current site in Asano. In 1931, it was designated a Prefectural Shrine. In 1995, the shrine's name was changed from "Tobata Hachiman Shrine" to "Tobihata Hachimangu." == festivals == The {{ill|Tobata Gion Yamakasa|ja|戸畑祇園大山笠|12=draft}} festival, held on the fourth weekend of July, is a major event at the shrine and at {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu)|ja|菅原神社 (北九州市戸畑区)|12=draft}} and Nakahara Hachiman Shrine in the ward. It is designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=draft}} of Japan. According to the {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記|12=draft}}, the festival began in Kyōwa 2 (1802) when an epidemic broke out in Tobata Village. After praying for healing at the shrine to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=draft}}, the epidemic ceased, and the festival was started the following year. == Gallery == {{gallery |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_01.jpg|First Torii |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_03.jpg|Shoichii Inari Daimyojin }} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]]]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Prefectural Shrines of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:History of Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category: [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Tobihata Hachimangu]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-27]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sv:Tobihata-hachimangū]] [[ja:飛幡八幡宮]] [[sn:Tobihata Hachimangu]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]] [[en:Tobihata Hachimangu]] [[sn:obihata Hachimangu]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tobihata Hachimangu}}

It was formerly ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|12=draft}} and is currently listed as a {{ill|Special Shrine|ja|別表神社|12=draft}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|12=draft}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|12=draft}} ({{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft}} and {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft}}), {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神|lt=Hime-no-Ōkami|1=Himegami|3=比売神|12=draft}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Dōsojin|ja|道祖神|12=draft}}. == History == In the Kenkyū period (1190-1199), {{ill|Utsunomiya Shigeie|ja|宇都宮重業|12=draft}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏|12=draft}} ({{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏|12=draft}}), brought the Hachiman deity from his hometown of Utsunomiya and enshrined it in Miyata Mountain in Edamitsu Village, which was the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門|12=draft}} (northeast) of {{ill|Hanao Castle|ja|花尾城|12=draft}}. Later, the shrine was moved to Tobata Village and became the guardian deity of Tobata and Nakahara Villages. During the Edo period, it was designated a prayer site by the {{ill|Kuroda clan|ja|黒田氏|12=draft}}. In 1920, it was relocated to its current site in Asano. In 1931, it was designated a Prefectural Shrine. In 1995, the shrine's name was changed from "Tobata Hachiman Shrine" to "Tobihata Hachimangu." == festivals == The {{ill|Tobata Gion Yamakasa|ja|戸畑祇園大山笠|12=draft}} festival, held on the fourth weekend of July, is a major event at the shrine and at {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu)|ja|菅原神社 (北九州市戸畑区)|12=draft}} and Nakahara Hachiman Shrine in the ward. It is designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=draft}} of Japan. According to the {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記|12=draft}}, the festival began in Kyōwa 2 (1802) when an epidemic broke out in Tobata Village. After praying for healing at the shrine to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=draft}}, the epidemic ceased, and the festival was started the following year. == Gallery == {{gallery |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_01.jpg|First Torii |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_03.jpg|Shoichii Inari Daimyojin }} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]]]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Prefectural Shrines of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:History of Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category: [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Tobihata Hachimangu]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sv:Tobihata-hachimangū]] [[ja:飛幡八幡宮]] [[sn:Tobihata Hachimangu]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]] [[en:Tobihata Hachimangu]] [[sn:obihata Hachimangu]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tobihata Hachimangu}}

After praying for healing at the shrine to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=draft}}, the epidemic ceased, and the festival was started the following year. == Gallery == {{gallery |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_01.jpg|First Torii |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_03.jpg|Shoichii Inari Daimyojin }} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]]]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Prefectural Shrines of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:History of Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category: [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Tobihata Hachimangu]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tobihata Hachimangu]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sv:Tobihata-hachimangū]] [[ja:飛幡八幡宮]] [[sn:Tobihata Hachimangu]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]] [[en:Tobihata Hachimangu]] [[sn:obihata Hachimangu]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tobihata Hachimangu}}

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Tobihata Hachimangu |image=[[File:Tobihata Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_02.jpg|300px]] |location= 2-2-2 Asano, Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu, Fukuoka Prefecture |position= {{Coord|33|53|36|N|130|49|38|E|region:JP-40_scale:30000|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft}}
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft}}
{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=draft}}
{{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神|lt=Hime-no-Ōkami|1=Himegami|3=比売神|12=draft}}
{{ill|Dōsojin|ja|道祖神|12=draft}} |rank= Prefectural Shrine, Special Shrine |founded= Kenkyū period (1190-1199) |festival= October 14 |rituals= Tobata Gion Yamakasa Event
Hōjōe }} '''Tobihata Hachimangu''' (飛幡八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu|ja|戸畑区|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kitakyushu|ja|北九州市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県|12=draft}}. It was formerly ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|12=draft}} and is currently listed as a {{ill|Special Shrine|ja|別表神社|12=draft}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|12=draft}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|12=draft}} ({{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft}} and {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft}}), {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神|lt=Hime-no-Ōkami|1=Himegami|3=比売神|12=draft}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Dōsojin|ja|道祖神|12=draft}}. == History == In the Kenkyū period (1190-1199), {{ill|Utsunomiya Shigeie|ja|宇都宮重業|12=draft}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏|12=draft}} ({{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏|12=draft}}), brought the Hachiman deity from his hometown of Utsunomiya and enshrined it in Miyata Mountain in Edamitsu Village, which was the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門|12=draft}} (northeast) of {{ill|Hanao Castle|ja|花尾城|12=draft}}. Later, the shrine was moved to Tobata Village and became the guardian deity of Tobata and Nakahara Villages. During the Edo period, it was designated a prayer site by the {{ill|Kuroda clan|ja|黒田氏|12=draft}}. In 1920, it was relocated to its current site in Asano. In 1931, it was designated a Prefectural Shrine. In 1995, the shrine's name was changed from "Tobata Hachiman Shrine" to "Tobihata Hachimangu." == festivals == The {{ill|Tobata Gion Yamakasa|ja|戸畑祇園大山笠|12=draft}} festival, held on the fourth weekend of July, is a major event at the shrine and at {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu)|ja|菅原神社 (北九州市戸畑区)|12=draft}} and Nakahara Hachiman Shrine in the ward. It is designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=draft}} of Japan. According to the {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記|12=draft}}, the festival began in Kyōwa 2 (1802) when an epidemic broke out in Tobata Village. After praying for healing at the shrine to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=draft}}, the epidemic ceased, and the festival was started the following year. == Gallery == {{gallery |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_01.jpg|First Torii |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_03.jpg|Shoichii Inari Daimyojin }} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]]]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Prefectural Shrines of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:History of Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category: [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Tobihata Hachimangu]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tobihata Hachimangu]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sv:Tobihata-hachimangū]] [[ja:飛幡八幡宮]] [[sn:Tobihata Hachimangu]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]] [[en:Tobihata Hachimangu]] [[sn:obihata Hachimangu]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tobihata Hachimangu}}

Hōjōe }} '''Tobihata Hachimangu''' (飛幡八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu|ja|戸畑区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kitakyushu|ja|北九州市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and is currently listed as a {{ill|Special Shrine|ja|別表神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神|lt=Hime-no-Ōkami|1=Himegami|3=比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Dōsojin|ja|道祖神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == History == In the Kenkyū period (1190-1199), {{ill|Utsunomiya Shigeie|ja|宇都宮重業|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), brought the Hachiman deity from his hometown of Utsunomiya and enshrined it in Miyata Mountain in Edamitsu Village, which was the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (northeast) of {{ill|Hanao Castle|ja|花尾城|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Later, the shrine was moved to Tobata Village and became the guardian deity of Tobata and Nakahara Villages. During the Edo period, it was designated a prayer site by the {{ill|Kuroda clan|ja|黒田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In 1920, it was relocated to its current site in Asano. In 1931, it was designated a Prefectural Shrine. In 1995, the shrine's name was changed from "Tobata Hachiman Shrine" to "Tobihata Hachimangu." == festivals == The {{ill|Tobata Gion Yamakasa|ja|戸畑祇園大山笠|qq=draft|qq=draft}} festival, held on the fourth weekend of July, is a major event at the shrine and at {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu)|ja|菅原神社 (北九州市戸畑区)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Nakahara Hachiman Shrine in the ward. It is designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of Japan. According to the {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the festival began in Kyōwa 2 (1802) when an epidemic broke out in Tobata Village. After praying for healing at the shrine to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the epidemic ceased, and the festival was started the following year. == Gallery == {{gallery |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_01.jpg|First Torii |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_03.jpg|Shoichii Inari Daimyojin }} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]]]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Prefectural Shrines of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:History of Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category: [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Tobihata Hachimangu]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tobihata Hachimangu]] [[sv:Tobihata-hachimangū]] [[ja:飛幡八幡宮]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]] [[en:Tobihata Hachimangu]] [[sn:obihata Hachimangu]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tobihata Hachimangu}}

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 飛幡八幡宮 |画像=[[ファイル:Tobihata Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_02.jpg|260px]]
拝殿 |所在地= [[福岡県]][[北九州市]][[戸畑区]][[浅生 (北九州市)|浅生]]2丁目2-2 |位置= {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|53|36.8|N|130|49|39|E|region:JP-40_scale:30000|display=inline,title}} |祭神= [[神功皇后]]
[[応神天皇]]
[[須佐之男命]]
[[比売大神]]
[[道祖神]] |社格= 旧[[県社]]・[[別表神社]] |創建= [[建久]]年間 |例祭= [[10月14日]] |神事= 戸畑祇園大山笠行事
放生会 |地図={{Location map|Japan Fukuoka|label=飛幡八幡宮|coordinates= {{coord2|33|53|36.8|N|130|49|39|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|53|36.8|N|130|49|39|E}}}} }} '''飛幡八幡宮'''（とびはたはちまんぐう）は、[[福岡県]][[北九州市]][[戸畑区]]にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]で、現在は[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。 == 祭神 == [[八幡神]]（[[神功皇后]]・[[応神天皇]]）・[[比売神|比売大神]]・[[スサノオ|須佐之男命]]・[[道祖神]]を祀る。 == 歴史 == [[建久]]年間（[[1190年]] - [[1199年]]）、筑前の[[宇都宮氏]]（[[麻生氏]]）の祖である[[宇都宮重業]]が、出身地の宇都宮から[[氏神]]の八幡神を勧請し、[[花尾城]]の[[鬼門]]にあたる枝光村宮田山に祀ったのに始まる。後に戸畑村に遷され、戸畑・中原両村の[[産土神]]とされた。江戸時代には藩主[[黒田氏|黒田家]]の[[祈願所]]とされた。[[1920年]]（大正9年）に現在地の[[浅生 (北九州市)|浅生]]に遷座した。[[1931年]]（昭和6年）に県社に列した。[[1995年]]（平成7年）、それまでの「戸畑八幡神社」から「飛幡八幡宮」に社名を変更した。 == 祭事 == 7月第4週金・土・日に行われる[[戸畑祇園大山笠]]は、当社および区内の[[菅原神社 (北九州市戸畑区)|菅原神社]]・中原八幡宮の祭りで、国の[[重要無形民俗文化財]]となっている。『[[筑前国続風土記]]』によれば、[[享和]]2年（[[1802年]]）、戸畑村内に疫病が蔓延し、飛幡八幡宮の[[須佐之男命]]に平癒を祈願した所終息したため、翌享和3年（[[1803年]]）から始められたものである。 == ギャラリー == {{gallery |ファイル:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_01.jpg|一の鳥居 |ファイル:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_03.jpg|正一位稲荷大明神 }} == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == * [https://tdetode.jimdofree.com/ 飛幡八幡宮] - 公式サイト {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とひはたはちまんくう}} [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:福岡県の旧県社]] [[Category:北九州市の神社]] [[Category:戸畑区の歴史]] [[Category:戸畑区の建築物]] [[Category:八幡宮]]

Hōjōe }} '''Tobihata Hachimangu''' (飛幡八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu|ja|戸畑区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kitakyushu|ja|北九州市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and is currently listed as a {{ill|Special Shrine|ja|別表神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神|lt=Hime-no-Ōkami|1=Himegami|3=比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Dōsojin|ja|道祖神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == History == In the Kenkyū period (1190-1199), {{ill|Utsunomiya Shigeie|ja|宇都宮重業|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), brought the Hachiman deity from his hometown of Utsunomiya and enshrined it in Miyata Mountain in Edamitsu Village, which was the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (northeast) of {{ill|Hanao Castle|ja|花尾城|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Later, the shrine was moved to Tobata Village and became the guardian deity of Tobata and Nakahara Villages. During the Edo period, it was designated a prayer site by the {{ill|Kuroda clan|ja|黒田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In 1920, it was relocated to its current site in Asano. In 1931, it was designated a Prefectural Shrine. In 1995, the shrine's name was changed from "Tobata Hachiman Shrine" to "Tobihata Hachimangu." == festivals == The {{ill|Tobata Gion Yamakasa|ja|戸畑祇園大山笠|qq=draft|qq=draft}} festival, held on the fourth weekend of July, is a major event at the shrine and at {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu)|ja|菅原神社 (北九州市戸畑区)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Nakahara Hachiman Shrine in the ward. It is designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of Japan. According to the {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the festival began in Kyōwa 2 (1802) when an epidemic broke out in Tobata Village. After praying for healing at the shrine to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the epidemic ceased, and the festival was started the following year. == Gallery == {{gallery |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_01.jpg|First Torii |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_03.jpg|Shoichii Inari Daimyojin }} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]]]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Prefectural Shrines of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:History of Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category: [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Tobihata Hachimangu]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tobihata Hachimangu]] [[sv:Tobihata-hachimangū]] [[ja:飛幡八幡宮]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]] [[en:Tobihata Hachimangu]] [[sn:obihata Hachimangu]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tobihata Hachimangu}} {{translated page|ja|飛幡八幡宮|version=102018923|comment=Imported full ja history}}

After praying for healing at the shrine to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the epidemic ceased, and the festival was started the following year. == Gallery == {{gallery |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_01.jpg|First Torii |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_03.jpg|Shoichii Inari Daimyojin }} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} ]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{DEFAULTSORT:Tobihata Hachimangu}} {{translated page|ja|飛幡八幡宮|version=102018923|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Prefectural Shrines of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:History of Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category: [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Tobihata Hachimangu]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]] [[sn:Tobihata Hachimangu]] [[sv:Tobihata-hachimangū]] [[ja:飛幡八幡宮]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]] [[en:Tobihata Hachimangu]] [[sn:obihata Hachimangu]]

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Tobihata Hachimangu |image=[[File:Tobihata Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_02.jpg|300px]] |location= 2-2-2 Asano, Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu, Fukuoka Prefecture |position= {{Coord|33|53|36|N|130|49|38|E|region:JP-40_scale:30000|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神|lt=Hime-no-Ōkami|1=Himegami|3=比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Dōsojin|ja|道祖神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Rank= Prefectural Shrine, Special Shrine |founded= Kenkyū period (1190-1199) |festival= October 14 |rituals= Tobata Gion Yamakasa Event
Hōjōe }} '''Tobihata Hachimangu''' (飛幡八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu|ja|戸畑区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kitakyushu|ja|北九州市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and is currently listed as a {{ill|Special Shrine|ja|別表神社|1=Beppyo Shrine|2=ja|3=別表神社|4=simple|5=Beppyo shrine|6=zh|7=別表神社|8=mr|9=बेप्प्यो तीर्थ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神|lt=Hime-no-Ōkami|1=Himegami|3=比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Dōsojin|ja|道祖神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == History == In the Kenkyū period (1190-1199), {{ill|Utsunomiya Shigeie|ja|宇都宮重業|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), brought the Hachiman deity from his hometown of Utsunomiya and enshrined it in Miyata Mountain in Edamitsu Village, which was the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (northeast) of {{ill|Hanao Castle|ja|花尾城|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Later, the shrine was moved to Tobata Village and became the guardian deity of Tobata and Nakahara Villages. During the Edo period, it was designated a prayer site by the {{ill|Kuroda clan|ja|黒田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In 1920, it was relocated to its current site in Asano. In 1931, it was designated a Prefectural Shrine. In 1995, the shrine's name was changed from "Tobata Hachiman Shrine" to "Tobihata Hachimangu." == festivals == The {{ill|Tobata Gion Yamakasa|ja|戸畑祇園大山笠|qq=draft|qq=draft}} festival, held on the fourth weekend of July, is a major event at the shrine and at {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu)|ja|菅原神社 (北九州市戸畑区)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Nakahara Hachiman Shrine in the ward. It is designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of Japan. According to the {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the festival began in Kyōwa 2 (1802) when an epidemic broke out in Tobata Village. After praying for healing at the shrine to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the epidemic ceased, and the festival was started the following year. == Gallery == {{gallery |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_01.jpg|First Torii |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_03.jpg|Shoichii Inari Daimyojin }} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} ]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{DEFAULTSORT:Tobihata Hachimangu}} {{translated page|ja|飛幡八幡宮|version=102018923|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Prefectural Shrines of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:History of Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category: [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Tobihata Hachimangu]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]] [[sn:Tobihata Hachimangu]] [[sv:Tobihata-hachimangū]] [[ja:飛幡八幡宮]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]] [[en:Tobihata Hachimangu]] [[sn:obihata Hachimangu]]

{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}}
{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神|lt=Hime-no-Ōkami|1=Himegami|3=比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|qq=}}
{{ill|Dōsojin|ja|道祖神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōsojin|qq=}} |Rank= Prefectural Shrine, Special Shrine |founded= Kenkyū period (1190-1199) |festival= October 14 |rituals= Tobata Gion Yamakasa Event
Hōjōe }} '''Tobihata Hachimangu''' (飛幡八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu|ja|戸畑区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu|qq=}}, {{ill|Kitakyushu|ja|北九州市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitakyushu|qq=}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefecture|qq=}}. It was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} and is currently listed as a {{ill|Special Shrine|ja|別表神社|1=Beppyo Shrine|2=ja|3=別表神社|4=simple|5=Beppyo shrine|6=zh|7=別表神社|8=mr|9=बेप्प्यो तीर्थ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppyo Shrine|qq=}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Association of Shinto Shrines|qq=}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman|qq=}} ({{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=}} and {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}}), {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神|lt=Hime-no-Ōkami|1=Himegami|3=比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|qq=}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, and {{ill|Dōsojin|ja|道祖神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōsojin|qq=}}. == History == In the Kenkyū period (1190-1199), {{ill|Utsunomiya Shigeie|ja|宇都宮重業|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shigeie|qq=}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya clan|qq=}} ({{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aso clan|qq=}}), brought the Hachiman deity from his hometown of Utsunomiya and enshrined it in Miyata Mountain in Edamitsu Village, which was the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimon|qq=}} (northeast) of {{ill|Hanao Castle|ja|花尾城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanao Castle|qq=}}. Later, the shrine was moved to Tobata Village and became the guardian deity of Tobata and Nakahara Villages. During the Edo period, it was designated a prayer site by the {{ill|Kuroda clan|ja|黒田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda clan|qq=}}. In 1920, it was relocated to its current site in Asano. In 1931, it was designated a Prefectural Shrine. In 1995, the shrine's name was changed from "Tobata Hachiman Shrine" to "Tobihata Hachimangu." == festivals == The {{ill|Tobata Gion Yamakasa|ja|戸畑祇園大山笠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tobata Gion Yamakasa|qq=}} festival, held on the fourth weekend of July, is a major event at the shrine and at {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu)|ja|菅原神社 (北九州市戸畑区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara Shrine (Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu)|qq=}} and Nakahara Hachiman Shrine in the ward. It is designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} of Japan. According to the {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki|qq=}}, the festival began in Kyōwa 2 (1802) when an epidemic broke out in Tobata Village. After praying for healing at the shrine to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, the epidemic ceased, and the festival was started the following year. == Gallery == {{gallery |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_01.jpg|First Torii |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_03.jpg|Shoichii Inari Daimyojin }} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} ]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|飛幡八幡宮|version=102018923|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Prefectural Shrines of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:History of Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category: [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Tobihata Hachimangu]] }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]] [[sn:Tobihata Hachimangu]] [[sv:Tobihata-hachimangū]] [[ja:飛幡八幡宮]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]] [[en:Tobihata Hachimangu]] [[sn:obihata Hachimangu]]

It was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} and is currently listed as a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] by the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. == Deities == The shrine enshrines [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] ([[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] and [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]), {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神|lt=Hime-no-Ōkami|1=Himegami|3=比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|qq=}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, and [[:en:Dōsojin|Dōsojin]]. == History == In the Kenkyū period (1190-1199), {{ill|Utsunomiya Shigeie|ja|宇都宮重業|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shigeie|qq=}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya clan|qq=}} ({{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aso clan|qq=}}), brought the Hachiman deity from his hometown of Utsunomiya and enshrined it in Miyata Mountain in Edamitsu Village, which was the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimon|qq=}} (northeast) of {{ill|Hanao Castle|ja|花尾城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanao Castle|qq=}}. Later, the shrine was moved to Tobata Village and became the guardian deity of Tobata and Nakahara Villages. During the Edo period, it was designated a prayer site by the [[:en:Kuroda clan|Kuroda clan]]. In 1920, it was relocated to its current site in Asano. In 1931, it was designated a Prefectural Shrine. In 1995, the shrine's name was changed from "Tobata Hachiman Shrine" to "Tobihata Hachimangu." == festivals == The [[:en:Tobata Gion Yamagasa festival|Tobata Gion Yamakasa]] festival, held on the fourth weekend of July, is a major event at the shrine and at {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu)|ja|菅原神社 (北九州市戸畑区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara Shrine (Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu)|qq=}} and Nakahara Hachiman Shrine in the ward. It is designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} of Japan. According to the {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki|qq=}}, the festival began in Kyōwa 2 (1802) when an epidemic broke out in Tobata Village. After praying for healing at the shrine to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, the epidemic ceased, and the festival was started the following year. == Gallery == {{gallery |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_01.jpg|First Torii |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_03.jpg|Shoichii Inari Daimyojin }} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} ]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|飛幡八幡宮|version=102018923|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Prefectural Shrines of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:History of Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category: [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]] [[sn:Tobihata Hachimangu]] [[sv:Tobihata-hachimangū]] [[ja:飛幡八幡宮]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]] [[en:Tobihata Hachimangu]] [[sn:obihata Hachimangu]]

During the Edo period, it was designated a prayer site by the [[:en:Kuroda clan|Kuroda clan]]. In 1920, it was relocated to its current site in Asano. In 1931, it was designated a Prefectural Shrine. In 1995, the shrine's name was changed from "Tobata Hachiman Shrine" to "Tobihata Hachimangu." == festivals == The [[:en:Tobata Gion Yamagasa festival|Tobata Gion Yamakasa]] festival, held on the fourth weekend of July, is a major event at the shrine and at {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu)|ja|菅原神社 (北九州市戸畑区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara Shrine (Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu)|qq=}} and Nakahara Hachiman Shrine in the ward. It was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} and is currently listed as a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] by the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. == Deities == The shrine enshrines [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] ([[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] and [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]), {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神|lt=Hime-no-Ōkami|1=Himegami|3=比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|qq=}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, and [[:en:Dōsojin|Dōsojin]]. == History == In the Kenkyū period (1190-1199), {{ill|Utsunomiya Shigeie|ja|宇都宮重業|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shigeie|qq=}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya clan|qq=}} ({{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aso clan|qq=}}), brought the Hachiman deity from his hometown of Utsunomiya and enshrined it in Miyata Mountain in Edamitsu Village, which was the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimon|qq=}} (northeast) of {{ill|Hanao Castle|ja|花尾城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanao Castle|qq=}}. Later, the shrine was moved to Tobata Village and became the guardian deity of Tobata and Nakahara Villages. During the Edo period, it was designated a prayer site by the [[:en:Kuroda clan|Kuroda clan]]. In 1920, it was relocated to its current site in Asano. In 1931, it was designated a Prefectural Shrine. In 1995, the shrine's name was changed from "Tobata Hachiman Shrine" to "Tobihata Hachimangu." == festivals == The [[:en:Tobata Gion Yamagasa festival|Tobata Gion Yamakasa]] festival, held on the fourth weekend of July, is a major event at the shrine and at {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu)|ja|菅原神社 (北九州市戸畑区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara Shrine (Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu)|qq=}} and Nakahara Hachiman Shrine in the ward. Hōjōe }} '''Tobihata Hachimangu''' (飛幡八幡宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tobata-ku, Kitakyūshū|Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu]], [[:en:Kitakyushu|Kitakyushu]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} and is currently listed as a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] by the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. == Deities == The shrine enshrines [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] ([[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] and [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]), {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神|lt=Hime-no-Ōkami|1=Himegami|3=比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|qq=}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, and [[:en:Dōsojin|Dōsojin]]. == History == In the Kenkyū period (1190-1199), {{ill|Utsunomiya Shigeie|ja|宇都宮重業|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shigeie|qq=}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya clan|qq=}} ({{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aso clan|qq=}}), brought the Hachiman deity from his hometown of Utsunomiya and enshrined it in Miyata Mountain in Edamitsu Village, which was the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimon|qq=}} (northeast) of {{ill|Hanao Castle|ja|花尾城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanao Castle|qq=}}. Later, the shrine was moved to Tobata Village and became the guardian deity of Tobata and Nakahara Villages. During the Edo period, it was designated a prayer site by the [[:en:Kuroda clan|Kuroda clan]]. In 1920, it was relocated to its current site in Asano. In 1931, it was designated a Prefectural Shrine. In 1995, the shrine's name was changed from "Tobata Hachiman Shrine" to "Tobihata Hachimangu." == festivals == The [[:en:Tobata Gion Yamagasa festival|Tobata Gion Yamakasa]] festival, held on the fourth weekend of July, is a major event at the shrine and at {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu)|ja|菅原神社 (北九州市戸畑区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara Shrine (Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu)|qq=}} and Nakahara Hachiman Shrine in the ward. It is designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} of Japan. According to the {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki|qq=}}, the festival began in Kyōwa 2 (1802) when an epidemic broke out in Tobata Village. After praying for healing at the shrine to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, the epidemic ceased, and the festival was started the following year. == Gallery == {{gallery |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_01.jpg|First Torii |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_03.jpg|Shoichii Inari Daimyojin }} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} ]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|飛幡八幡宮|version=102018923|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Prefectural Shrines of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:History of Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category: [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]] [[sn:Tobihata Hachimangu]] [[sv:Tobihata-hachimangū]] [[ja:飛幡八幡宮]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]] [[sn:obihata Hachimangu]] [[Category:Shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Buildings in Tobata-ku, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:戸畑区の歴史]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Tobihata Hachiman-gū}}

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Tobihata Hachimangu |image=[[File:Tobihata Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_02.jpg|300px]] |location= 2-2-2 Asano, Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu, Fukuoka Prefecture |position= {{Coord|33|53|36|N|130|49|38|E|region:JP-40_scale:30000|display=inline,title}} |deity= [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]
[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]
{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}
{{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神|lt=Hime-no-Ōkami|1=Himegami|3=比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}}
[[:en:Dōsojin|Dōsojin]] |Rank= Prefectural Shrine, Special Shrine |founded= Kenkyū period (1190-1199) |festival= October 14 |rituals= Tobata Gion Yamakasa Event
Hōjōe }} '''Tobihata Hachimangu''' (飛幡八幡宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tobata-ku, Kitakyūshū|Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu]], [[:en:Kitakyushu|Kitakyushu]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} and is currently listed as a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] by the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. == Deities == The shrine enshrines [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] ([[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] and [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]), {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神|lt=Hime-no-Ōkami|1=Himegami|3=比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, and [[:en:Dōsojin|Dōsojin]]. == History == In the Kenkyū period (1190-1199), {{ill|Utsunomiya Shigeie|ja|宇都宮重業|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shigeie|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya clan|qq=}} ({{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aso clan|qq=}}), brought the Hachiman deity from his hometown of Utsunomiya and enshrined it in Miyata Mountain in Edamitsu Village, which was the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimon|qq=}} (northeast) of {{ill|Hanao Castle|ja|花尾城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanao Castle|qq=}}. Later, the shrine was moved to Tobata Village and became the guardian deity of Tobata and Nakahara Villages. During the Edo period, it was designated a prayer site by the [[:en:Kuroda clan|Kuroda clan]]. In 1920, it was relocated to its current site in Asano. In 1931, it was designated a Prefectural Shrine. In 1995, the shrine's name was changed from "Tobata Hachiman Shrine" to "Tobihata Hachimangu." == festivals == The [[:en:Tobata Gion Yamagasa festival|Tobata Gion Yamakasa]] festival, held on the fourth weekend of July, is a major event at the shrine and at {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu)|ja|菅原神社 (北九州市戸畑区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara Shrine (Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu)|qq=}} and Nakahara Hachiman Shrine in the ward. It is designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} of Japan. According to the {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki|qq=}}, the festival began in Kyōwa 2 (1802) when an epidemic broke out in Tobata Village. After praying for healing at the shrine to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, the epidemic ceased, and the festival was started the following year. == Gallery == {{gallery |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_01.jpg|First Torii |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_03.jpg|Shoichii Inari Daimyojin }} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} ]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|飛幡八幡宮|version=102018923|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Prefectural Shrines of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category: [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]] [[sn:Tobihata Hachimangu]] [[sv:Tobihata-hachimangū]] [[ja:飛幡八幡宮]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]] [[sn:obihata Hachimangu]] [[Category:Shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Buildings in Tobata-ku, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:戸畑区の歴史]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Tobihata Hachiman-gū}}

After praying for healing at the shrine to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, the epidemic ceased, and the festival was started the following year. == Gallery == {{gallery |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_01.jpg|First Torii |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_03.jpg|Shoichii Inari Daimyojin }} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} ]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|飛幡八幡宮|version=102018923|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category: [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]] [[sn:Tobihata Hachimangu]] [[sv:Tobihata-hachimangū]] [[ja:飛幡八幡宮]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]] [[sn:obihata Hachimangu]] [[Category:Shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Buildings in Tobata-ku, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:戸畑区の歴史]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Tobihata Hachiman-gū}}

Hōjōe }} '''Tobihata Hachimangu''' (飛幡八幡宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tobata-ku, Kitakyūshū|Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu]], [[:en:Kitakyushu|Kitakyushu]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} and is currently listed as a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] by the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. == Deities == The shrine enshrines [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] ([[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] and [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]), {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神|lt=Hime-no-Ōkami|1=Himegami|3=比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, and [[:en:Dōsojin|Dōsojin]]. == History == In the Kenkyū period (1190-1199), {{ill|Utsunomiya Shigeie|ja|宇都宮重業|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shigeie|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya clan|qq=}} ({{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aso clan|qq=}}), brought the Hachiman deity from his hometown of Utsunomiya and enshrined it in Miyata Mountain in Edamitsu Village, which was the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門|1=Kimon|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimon|qq=}} (northeast) of {{ill|Hanao Castle|ja|花尾城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanao Castle|qq=}}. Later, the shrine was moved to Tobata Village and became the guardian deity of Tobata and Nakahara Villages. During the Edo period, it was designated a prayer site by the [[:en:Kuroda clan|Kuroda clan]]. In 1920, it was relocated to its current site in Asano. In 1931, it was designated a Prefectural Shrine. In 1995, the shrine's name was changed from "Tobata Hachiman Shrine" to "Tobihata Hachimangu." == festivals == The [[:en:Tobata Gion Yamagasa festival|Tobata Gion Yamakasa]] festival, held on the fourth weekend of July, is a major event at the shrine and at {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu)|ja|菅原神社 (北九州市戸畑区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara Shrine (Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu)|qq=}} and Nakahara Hachiman Shrine in the ward. It is designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} of Japan. According to the {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki|qq=}}, the festival began in Kyōwa 2 (1802) when an epidemic broke out in Tobata Village. After praying for healing at the shrine to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, the epidemic ceased, and the festival was started the following year. == Gallery == {{gallery |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_01.jpg|First Torii |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_03.jpg|Shoichii Inari Daimyojin }} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} ]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|飛幡八幡宮|version=102018923|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category: [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]] [[sn:Tobihata Hachimangu]] [[sv:Tobihata-hachimangū]] [[ja:飛幡八幡宮]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]] [[sn:obihata Hachimangu]] [[Category:Shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Buildings in Tobata-ku, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:戸畑区の歴史]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Tobihata Hachiman-gū}}

During the Edo period, it was designated a prayer site by the [[:en:Kuroda clan|Kuroda clan]]. In 1920, it was relocated to its current site in Asano. In 1931, it was designated a Prefectural Shrine. In 1995, the shrine's name was changed from "Tobata Hachiman Shrine" to "Tobihata Hachimangu." == festivals == The [[:en:Tobata Gion Yamagasa festival|Tobata Gion Yamakasa]] festival, held on the fourth weekend of July, is a major event at the shrine and at {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu)|ja|菅原神社 (北九州市戸畑区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara Shrine (Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu)|qq=}} and Nakahara Hachiman Shrine in the ward. It is designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} of Japan. According to the {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki|qq=}}, the festival began in Kyōwa 2 (1802) when an epidemic broke out in Tobata Village. After praying for healing at the shrine to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, the epidemic ceased, and the festival was started the following year. == Gallery == {{gallery |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_01.jpg|First Torii |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_03.jpg|Shoichii Inari Daimyojin }} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|飛幡八幡宮|version=102018923|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category: [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]] [[sn:Tobihata Hachimangu]] [[sv:Tobihata-hachimangū]] [[ja:飛幡八幡宮]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]] [[sn:obihata Hachimangu]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Buildings in Tobata-ku, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:History of Tobata Ward]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Tobihata Hachiman-gū}}

Later, the shrine was moved to Tobata Village and became the guardian deity of Tobata and Nakahara Villages. During the Edo period, it was designated a prayer site by the [[:en:Kuroda clan|Kuroda clan]]. In 1920, it was relocated to its current site in Asano. In 1931, it was designated a Prefectural Shrine. In 1995, the shrine's name was changed from "Tobata Hachiman Shrine" to "Tobihata Hachimangu." == festivals == The [[:en:Tobata Gion Yamagasa festival|Tobata Gion Yamakasa]] festival, held on the fourth weekend of July, is a major event at the shrine and at {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu)|ja|菅原神社 (北九州市戸畑区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara Shrine (Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu)|qq=}} and Nakahara Hachiman Shrine in the ward. After praying for healing at the shrine to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, the epidemic ceased, and the festival was started the following year. == Gallery == {{gallery |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_01.jpg|First Torii |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_03.jpg|Shoichii Inari Daimyojin }} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|飛幡八幡宮|version=102018923|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category: [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]] [[sn:Tobihata Hachimangu]] [[sv:Tobihata-hachimangū]] [[ja:飛幡八幡宮]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]] [[sn:obihata Hachimangu]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Buildings in Tobata-ku, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:History of Tobata Ward]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Tobihata Hachiman-gū}}

It is designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} of Japan. According to the {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki|qq=|1=Chikuzen'nokuni Zoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108571667}}, the festival began in Kyōwa 2 (1802) when an epidemic broke out in Tobata Village. After praying for healing at the shrine to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, the epidemic ceased, and the festival was started the following year. == Gallery == {{gallery |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_01.jpg|First Torii |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_03.jpg|Shoichii Inari Daimyojin }} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|飛幡八幡宮|version=102018923|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category: [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sv:Tobihata-hachimangū]] [[ja:飛幡八幡宮]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Buildings in Tobata-ku, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:History of Tobata Ward]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Tobihata Hachiman-gū}} {{wikidata link|Q11665771}}

It was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and is currently listed as a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] by the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. == Deities == The shrine enshrines [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] ([[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] and [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]), {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神|lt=Hime-no-Ōkami|1=Himegami|3=比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, and [[:en:Dōsojin|Dōsojin]]. == History == In the Kenkyū period (1190-1199), {{ill|Utsunomiya Shigeie|ja|宇都宮重業|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shigeie|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya clan|qq=|1=Utsunomiya clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449780}} ({{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aso clan|qq=}}), brought the Hachiman deity from his hometown of Utsunomiya and enshrined it in Miyata Mountain in Edamitsu Village, which was the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門|1=Kimon|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimon|qq=}} (northeast) of {{ill|Hanao Castle|ja|花尾城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanao Castle|qq=|1=Hanao Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615260}}. Later, the shrine was moved to Tobata Village and became the guardian deity of Tobata and Nakahara Villages. During the Edo period, it was designated a prayer site by the [[:en:Kuroda clan|Kuroda clan]]. In 1920, it was relocated to its current site in Asano. In 1931, it was designated a Prefectural Shrine. In 1995, the shrine's name was changed from "Tobata Hachiman Shrine" to "Tobihata Hachimangu." == festivals == The [[:en:Tobata Gion Yamagasa festival|Tobata Gion Yamakasa]] festival, held on the fourth weekend of July, is a major event at the shrine and at {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu)|ja|菅原神社 (北九州市戸畑区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara Shrine (Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu)|qq=|1=Sugawara Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17218762}} and Nakahara Hachiman Shrine in the ward. It is designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} of Japan. After praying for healing at the shrine to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, the epidemic ceased, and the festival was started the following year. == Gallery == {{gallery |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_01.jpg|First Torii |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_03.jpg|Shoichii Inari Daimyojin }} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|飛幡八幡宮|version=102018923|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category: [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sv:Tobihata-hachimangū]] [[ja:飛幡八幡宮]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Buildings in Tobata-ku, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:History of Tobata Ward]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Tobihata Hachiman-gū}} {{wikidata link|Q11665771}}

[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]
{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}
{{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神|lt=Hime-no-Ōkami|1=Himegami|3=比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}}

Hōjōe |prefecture= {{ill|Tobata-ku|WD=Q858143}} |coordinates= {{Coord|33.893333|N|130.827222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Tobihata Hachimangu''' (飛幡八幡宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tobata-ku, Kitakyūshū|Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu]], [[:en:Kitakyushu|Kitakyushu]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and is currently listed as a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] by the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. == Deities == The shrine enshrines [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] ([[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] and [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]), {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神|lt=Hime-no-Ōkami|1=Himegami|3=比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, and [[:en:Dōsojin|Dōsojin]]. == History == In the Kenkyū period (1190-1199), {{ill|Utsunomiya Shigeie|ja|宇都宮重業|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shigeie|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya clan|1=Utsunomiya clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449780}} ({{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aso clan|qq=}}), brought the Hachiman deity from his hometown of Utsunomiya and enshrined it in Miyata Mountain in Edamitsu Village, which was the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門|1=Kimon|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimon|qq=}} (northeast) of {{ill|Hanao Castle|ja|花尾城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanao Castle|1=Hanao Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615260}}. Later, the shrine was moved to Tobata Village and became the guardian deity of Tobata and Nakahara Villages. During the Edo period, it was designated a prayer site by the [[:en:Kuroda clan|Kuroda clan]]. In 1920, it was relocated to its current site in Asano. In 1931, it was designated a Prefectural Shrine. In 1995, the shrine's name was changed from "Tobata Hachiman Shrine" to "Tobihata Hachimangu." == festivals == The [[:en:Tobata Gion Yamagasa festival|Tobata Gion Yamakasa]] festival, held on the fourth weekend of July, is a major event at the shrine and at {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu)|ja|菅原神社 (北九州市戸畑区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara Shrine (Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu)|1=Sugawara Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17218762}} and Nakahara Hachiman Shrine in the ward. It is designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} of Japan. According to the {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki|1=Chikuzen'nokuni Zoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108571667}}, the festival began in Kyōwa 2 (1802) when an epidemic broke out in Tobata Village. After praying for healing at the shrine to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, the epidemic ceased, and the festival was started the following year. == Gallery == {{gallery |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_01.jpg|First Torii |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_03.jpg|Shoichii Inari Daimyojin }} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|飛幡八幡宮|version=102018923|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category: [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sv:Tobihata-hachimangū]] [[ja:飛幡八幡宮]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Buildings in Tobata-ku, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:History of Tobata Ward]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Tobihata Hachiman-gū}} {{wikidata link|Q11665771}}

It was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and is currently listed as a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] by the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. == Deities == The shrine enshrines [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] ([[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] and [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]), {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神|lt=Hime-no-Ōkami|1=Himegami|3=比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, and [[:en:Dōsojin|Dōsojin]]. == History == In the Kenkyū period (1190-1199), {{ill|Utsunomiya Shigeie|ja|宇都宮重業|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shigeie|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya clan|1=Utsunomiya clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449780}} ({{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aso clan|qq=}}), brought the Hachiman deity from his hometown of Utsunomiya and enshrined it in Miyata Mountain in Edamitsu Village, which was the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門|1=Kimon|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimon|qq=}} (northeast) of {{ill|Hanao Castle|ja|花尾城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanao Castle|1=Hanao Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615260}}. Later, the shrine was moved to Tobata Village and became the guardian deity of Tobata and Nakahara Villages. During the Edo period, it was designated a prayer site by the [[:en:Kuroda clan|Kuroda clan]]. In 1920, it was relocated to its current site in Asano. In 1931, it was designated a Prefectural Shrine. In 1995, the shrine's name was changed from "Tobata Hachiman Shrine" to "Tobihata Hachimangu." == festivals == The [[:en:Tobata Gion Yamagasa festival|Tobata Gion Yamakasa]] festival, held on the fourth weekend of July, is a major event at the shrine and at {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu)|ja|菅原神社 (北九州市戸畑区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara Shrine (Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu)|1=Sugawara Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17218762}} and Nakahara Hachiman Shrine in the ward. According to the {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki|1=Chikuzen'nokuni Zoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108571667}}, the festival began in Kyōwa 2 (1802) when an epidemic broke out in Tobata Village. After praying for healing at the shrine to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, the epidemic ceased, and the festival was started the following year. == Gallery == {{gallery |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_01.jpg|First Torii |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_03.jpg|Shoichii Inari Daimyojin }} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|飛幡八幡宮|version=102018923|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category: [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sv:Tobihata-hachimangū]] [[ja:飛幡八幡宮]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Buildings in Tobata-ku, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:History of Tobata Ward]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Tobihata Hachiman-gū}} {{wikidata link|Q11665771}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
