According to the {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki|1=Chikuzen'nokuni Zoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108571667}}, the festival began in Kyōwa 2 (1802) when an epidemic broke out in Tobata Village. After praying for healing at the shrine to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, the epidemic ceased, and the festival was started the following year. == Gallery == {{gallery |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_01.jpg|First Torii |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_03.jpg|Shoichii Inari Daimyojin }} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|飛幡八幡宮|version=102018923|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category: [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sv:Tobihata-hachimangū]] [[ja:飛幡八幡宮]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Buildings in Tobata-ku, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:History of Tobata Ward]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Tobihata Hachiman-gū}} {{wikidata link|Q11665771}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11665771}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]
{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}
{{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神|lt=Hime-no-Ōkami|1=Himegami|3=比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}}

Hōjōe |prefecture= {{ill|Tobata-ku|WD=Q858143}} |coordinates= {{Coord|33.893333|N|130.827222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Tobihata Hachimangu''' (飛幡八幡宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tobata-ku, Kitakyūshū|Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu]], [[:en:Kitakyushu|Kitakyushu]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and is currently listed as a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] by the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. == Deities == The shrine enshrines [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] ([[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] and [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]), {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神|lt=Hime-no-Ōkami|1=Himegami|3=比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, and [[:en:Dōsojin|Dōsojin]]. == History == In the Kenkyū period (1190-1199), {{ill|Utsunomiya Shigeie|ja|宇都宮重業|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shigeie|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya clan|1=Utsunomiya clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449780}} ({{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aso clan|qq=}}), brought the Hachiman deity from his hometown of Utsunomiya and enshrined it in Miyata Mountain in Edamitsu Village, which was the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門|1=Kimon|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimon|qq=}} (northeast) of {{ill|Hanao Castle|ja|花尾城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanao Castle|1=Hanao Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615260}}. Later, the shrine was moved to Tobata Village and became the guardian deity of Tobata and Nakahara Villages. During the Edo period, it was designated a prayer site by the [[:en:Kuroda clan|Kuroda clan]]. In 1920, it was relocated to its current site in Asano. In 1931, it was designated a Prefectural Shrine. In 1995, the shrine's name was changed from "Tobata Hachiman Shrine" to "Tobihata Hachimangu." == festivals == The [[:en:Tobata Gion Yamagasa festival|Tobata Gion Yamakasa]] festival, held on the fourth weekend of July, is a major event at the shrine and at {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu)|ja|菅原神社 (北九州市戸畑区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara Shrine (Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu)|1=Sugawara Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17218762}} and Nakahara Hachiman Shrine in the ward. It is designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} of Japan. According to the {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki|1=Chikuzen'nokuni Zoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108571667}}, the festival began in Kyōwa 2 (1802) when an epidemic broke out in Tobata Village. After praying for healing at the shrine to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, the epidemic ceased, and the festival was started the following year. == Gallery == {{gallery |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_01.jpg|First Torii |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_03.jpg|Shoichii Inari Daimyojin }} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|飛幡八幡宮|version=102018923|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category: [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sv:Tobihata-hachimangū]] [[ja:飛幡八幡宮]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Buildings in Tobata-ku, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:History of Tobata Ward]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Tobihata Hachiman-gū}} {{wikidata link|Q11665771}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11665771}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}
{{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神|lt=Hime-no-Ōkami|1=Himegami|3=比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}}
[[:en:Dōsojin|Dōsojin]] |Rank= Prefectural Shrine, Special Shrine |founded= Kenkyū period (1190-1199) |festival= October 14 |rituals= Tobata Gion Yamakasa Event
Hōjōe |prefecture= {{ill|Tobata-ku|WD=Q858143}} |coordinates= {{Coord|33.893333|N|130.827222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Tobihata Hachimangu''' (飛幡八幡宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tobata-ku, Kitakyūshū|Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu]], [[:en:Kitakyushu|Kitakyushu]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and is currently listed as a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] by the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. == Deities == The shrine enshrines [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] ([[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] and [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]), {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神|lt=Hime-no-Ōkami|1=Himegami|3=比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, and [[:en:Dōsojin|Dōsojin]]. == History == In the Kenkyū period (1190-1199), {{ill|Utsunomiya Shigeie|ja|宇都宮重業|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shigeie|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya clan|1=Utsunomiya clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449780}} ({{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aso clan|qq=}}), brought the Hachiman deity from his hometown of Utsunomiya and enshrined it in Miyata Mountain in Edamitsu Village, which was the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門|1=Kimon|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimon|qq=}} (northeast) of {{ill|Hanao Castle|ja|花尾城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanao Castle|1=Hanao Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615260}}. Later, the shrine was moved to Tobata Village and became the guardian deity of Tobata and Nakahara Villages. During the Edo period, it was designated a prayer site by the [[:en:Kuroda clan|Kuroda clan]]. In 1920, it was relocated to its current site in Asano. In 1931, it was designated a Prefectural Shrine. In 1995, the shrine's name was changed from "Tobata Hachiman Shrine" to "Tobihata Hachimangu." == festivals == The [[:en:Tobata Gion Yamagasa festival|Tobata Gion Yamakasa]] festival, held on the fourth weekend of July, is a major event at the shrine and at {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu)|ja|菅原神社 (北九州市戸畑区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara Shrine (Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu)|1=Sugawara Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17218762}} and Nakahara Hachiman Shrine in the ward. It is designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} of Japan. According to the {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki|1=Chikuzen'nokuni Zoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108571667}}, the festival began in Kyōwa 2 (1802) when an epidemic broke out in Tobata Village. After praying for healing at the shrine to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, the epidemic ceased, and the festival was started the following year. == Gallery == {{gallery |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_01.jpg|First Torii |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_03.jpg|Shoichii Inari Daimyojin }} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|飛幡八幡宮|version=102018923|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category: [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sv:Tobihata-hachimangū]] [[ja:飛幡八幡宮]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Buildings in Tobata-ku, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:History of Tobata Ward]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Tobihata Hachiman-gū}} {{wikidata link|Q11665771}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11665771}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]]

It was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and is currently listed as a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] by the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. == Deities == The shrine enshrines [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] ([[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] and [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]), {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神|lt=Hime-no-Ōkami|1=Himegami|3=比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, and [[:en:Dōsojin|Dōsojin]]. == History == In the Kenkyū period (1190-1199), {{ill|Utsunomiya Shigeie|ja|宇都宮重業|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shigeie|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya clan|1=Utsunomiya clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449780}} ({{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aso clan|qq=}}), brought the Hachiman deity from his hometown of Utsunomiya and enshrined it in Miyata Mountain in Edamitsu Village, which was the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門|1=Kimon|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimon|qq=}} (northeast) of {{ill|Hanao Castle|ja|花尾城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanao Castle|1=Hanao Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615260}}. Later, the shrine was moved to Tobata Village and became the guardian deity of Tobata and Nakahara Villages. During the Edo period, it was designated a prayer site by the [[:en:Kuroda clan|Kuroda clan]]. In 1920, it was relocated to its current site in Asano. In 1931, it was designated a Prefectural Shrine. In 1995, the shrine's name was changed from "Tobata Hachiman Shrine" to "Tobihata Hachimangu." == festivals == The [[:en:Tobata Gion Yamagasa festival|Tobata Gion Yamakasa]] festival, held on the fourth weekend of July, is a major event at the shrine and at {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu)|ja|菅原神社 (北九州市戸畑区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara Shrine (Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu)|1=Sugawara Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17218762}} and Nakahara Hachiman Shrine in the ward. It is designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} of Japan. According to the {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki|1=Chikuzen'nokuni Zoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108571667}}, the festival began in Kyōwa 2 (1802) when an epidemic broke out in Tobata Village. After praying for healing at the shrine to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, the epidemic ceased, and the festival was started the following year. == Gallery == {{gallery |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_01.jpg|First Torii |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_03.jpg|Shoichii Inari Daimyojin }} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|飛幡八幡宮|version=102018923|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category: [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sv:Tobihata-hachimangū]] [[ja:飛幡八幡宮]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Buildings in Tobata-ku, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:History of Tobata Ward]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Tobihata Hachiman-gū}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] {{wikidata link|Q11665771}}

According to the {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki|1=Chikuzen'nokuni Zoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108571667}}, the festival began in Kyōwa 2 (1802) when an epidemic broke out in Tobata Village. After praying for healing at the shrine to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, the epidemic ceased, and the festival was started the following year. == Gallery == {{gallery |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_01.jpg|First Torii |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_03.jpg|Shoichii Inari Daimyojin }} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|飛幡八幡宮|version=102018923|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category: [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sv:Tobihata-hachimangū]] [[ja:飛幡八幡宮]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Buildings in Tobata-ku, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:History of Tobata Ward]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Tobihata Hachiman-gū}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11665771}}

Hōjōe |prefecture= {{ill|Tobata-ku|WD=Q858143}} |coordinates= {{Coord|33.893333|N|130.827222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Tobihata Hachimangu''' (飛幡八幡宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tobata-ku, Kitakyūshū|Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu]], [[:en:Kitakyushu|Kitakyushu]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and is currently listed as a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] by the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. == Deities == The shrine enshrines [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] ([[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] and [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]), {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神|lt=Hime-no-Ōkami|1=Himegami|3=比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, and [[:en:Dōsojin|Dōsojin]]. == History == In the Kenkyū period (1190-1199), {{ill|Utsunomiya Shigeie|ja|宇都宮重業|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shigeie|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya clan|1=Utsunomiya clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449780}} ({{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aso clan|qq=}}), brought the Hachiman deity from his hometown of Utsunomiya and enshrined it in Miyata Mountain in Edamitsu Village, which was the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門|1=Kimon|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimon|qq=}} (northeast) of {{ill|Hanao Castle|ja|花尾城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanao Castle|1=Hanao Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615260}}. Later, the shrine was moved to Tobata Village and became the guardian deity of Tobata and Nakahara Villages. During the Edo period, it was designated a prayer site by the [[:en:Kuroda clan|Kuroda clan]]. In 1920, it was relocated to its current site in Asano. In 1931, it was designated a Prefectural Shrine. In 1995, the shrine's name was changed from "Tobata Hachiman Shrine" to "Tobihata Hachimangu." == festivals == The [[:en:Tobata Gion Yamagasa festival|Tobata Gion Yamakasa]] festival, held on the fourth weekend of July, is a major event at the shrine and at {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu)|ja|菅原神社 (北九州市戸畑区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara Shrine (Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu)|1=Sugawara Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17218762}} and Nakahara Hachiman Shrine in the ward. It is designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} of Japan. According to the {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki|1=Chikuzen'nokuni Zoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108571667}}, the festival began in Kyōwa 2 (1802) when an epidemic broke out in Tobata Village. After praying for healing at the shrine to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, the epidemic ceased, and the festival was started the following year. == Gallery == {{gallery |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_01.jpg|First Torii |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_03.jpg|Shoichii Inari Daimyojin }} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|飛幡八幡宮|version=102018923|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category: [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sv:Tobihata-hachimangū]] [[ja:飛幡八幡宮]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Buildings in Tobata-ku, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:History of Tobata Ward]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Tobihata Hachiman-gū}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11665771}}

{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}
{{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神|lt=Hime-no-Ōkami|1=Himegami|3=比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}}
[[:en:Dōsojin|Dōsojin]] |Rank= Prefectural Shrine, Special Shrine |founded= Kenkyū period (1190-1199) |festival= October 14 |rituals= Tobata Gion Yamakasa Event
Hōjōe |prefecture= {{ill|Tobata-ku|WD=Q858143}} |coordinates= {{Coord|33.893333|N|130.827222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Tobihata Hachimangu''' (飛幡八幡宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tobata-ku, Kitakyūshū|Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu]], [[:en:Kitakyushu|Kitakyushu]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and is currently listed as a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] by the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. == Deities == The shrine enshrines [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] ([[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] and [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]), {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神|lt=Hime-no-Ōkami|1=Himegami|3=比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, and [[:en:Dōsojin|Dōsojin]]. == History == In the Kenkyū period (1190-1199), {{ill|Utsunomiya Shigeie|ja|宇都宮重業|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shigeie|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya clan|1=Utsunomiya clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449780}} ({{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aso clan|qq=}}), brought the Hachiman deity from his hometown of Utsunomiya and enshrined it in Miyata Mountain in Edamitsu Village, which was the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門|1=Kimon|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimon|qq=}} (northeast) of {{ill|Hanao Castle|ja|花尾城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanao Castle|1=Hanao Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615260}}. Later, the shrine was moved to Tobata Village and became the guardian deity of Tobata and Nakahara Villages. During the Edo period, it was designated a prayer site by the [[:en:Kuroda clan|Kuroda clan]]. In 1920, it was relocated to its current site in Asano. In 1931, it was designated a Prefectural Shrine. In 1995, the shrine's name was changed from "Tobata Hachiman Shrine" to "Tobihata Hachimangu." == festivals == The [[:en:Tobata Gion Yamagasa festival|Tobata Gion Yamakasa]] festival, held on the fourth weekend of July, is a major event at the shrine and at {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu)|ja|菅原神社 (北九州市戸畑区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara Shrine (Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu)|1=Sugawara Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17218762}} and Nakahara Hachiman Shrine in the ward. It is designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} of Japan. According to the {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki|1=Chikuzen'nokuni Zoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108571667}}, the festival began in Kyōwa 2 (1802) when an epidemic broke out in Tobata Village. After praying for healing at the shrine to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, the epidemic ceased, and the festival was started the following year. == Gallery == {{gallery |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_01.jpg|First Torii |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_03.jpg|Shoichii Inari Daimyojin }} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|飛幡八幡宮|version=102018923|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[sv:Tobihata-hachimangū]] [[ja:飛幡八幡宮]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]] {{Commons category|Tobihata Hachiman-gū}} {{wikidata link|Q11665771}} [[Category: [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Tobata-ku, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:History of Tobata Ward]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[:en:Dōsojin|Dōsojin]] |Rank= Prefectural Shrine, Special Shrine |founded= Kenkyū period (1190-1199) |festival= October 14 |rituals= Tobata Gion Yamakasa Event
Hōjōe |prefecture= {{ill|Tobata-ku|WD=Q858143}} |coordinates= {{Coord|33.893333|N|130.827222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Tobihata Hachimangu''' (飛幡八幡宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tobata-ku, Kitakyūshū|Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu]], [[:en:Kitakyushu|Kitakyushu]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and is currently listed as a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] by the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. == Deities == The shrine enshrines [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] ([[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] and [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]), {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神|lt=Hime-no-Ōkami|1=Himegami|3=比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, and [[:en:Dōsojin|Dōsojin]]. == History == In the Kenkyū period (1190-1199), {{ill|Utsunomiya Shigeie|ja|宇都宮重業|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shigeie|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya clan|1=Utsunomiya clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449780}} ({{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aso clan|qq=}}), brought the Hachiman deity from his hometown of Utsunomiya and enshrined it in Miyata Mountain in Edamitsu Village, which was the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門|1=Kimon|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimon|qq=}} (northeast) of {{ill|Hanao Castle|ja|花尾城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanao Castle|1=Hanao Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615260}}. Later, the shrine was moved to Tobata Village and became the guardian deity of Tobata and Nakahara Villages. During the Edo period, it was designated a prayer site by the [[:en:Kuroda clan|Kuroda clan]]. In 1920, it was relocated to its current site in Asano. In 1931, it was designated a Prefectural Shrine. In 1995, the shrine's name was changed from "Tobata Hachiman Shrine" to "Tobihata Hachimangu." == festivals == The [[:en:Tobata Gion Yamagasa festival|Tobata Gion Yamakasa]] festival, held on the fourth weekend of July, is a major event at the shrine and at {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu)|ja|菅原神社 (北九州市戸畑区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara Shrine (Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu)|1=Sugawara Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17218762}} and Nakahara Hachiman Shrine in the ward. It is designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} of Japan. According to the {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki|1=Chikuzen'nokuni Zoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108571667}}, the festival began in Kyōwa 2 (1802) when an epidemic broke out in Tobata Village. After praying for healing at the shrine to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, the epidemic ceased, and the festival was started the following year. == Gallery == {{gallery |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_01.jpg|First Torii |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_03.jpg|Shoichii Inari Daimyojin }} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|飛幡八幡宮|version=102018923|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[sv:Tobihata-hachimangū]] [[ja:飛幡八幡宮]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]] {{Commons category|Tobihata Hachiman-gū}} {{wikidata link|Q11665771}} [[Category: [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Tobata-ku, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:History of Tobata Ward]] [[Category:Qq]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Later, the shrine was moved to Tobata Village and became the guardian deity of Tobata and Nakahara Villages. During the Edo period, it was designated a prayer site by the [[:en:Kuroda clan|Kuroda clan]]. In 1920, it was relocated to its current site in Asano. In 1931, it was designated a Prefectural Shrine. In 1995, the shrine's name was changed from "Tobata Hachiman Shrine" to "Tobihata Hachimangu." == festivals == The [[:en:Tobata Gion Yamagasa festival|Tobata Gion Yamakasa]] festival, held on the fourth weekend of July, is a major event at the shrine and at {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu)|ja|菅原神社 (北九州市戸畑区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara Shrine (Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu)|1=Sugawara Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17218762}} and Nakahara Hachiman Shrine in the ward. It is designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} of Japan. According to the {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki|1=Chikuzen'nokuni Zoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108571667}}, the festival began in Kyōwa 2 (1802) when an epidemic broke out in Tobata Village. After praying for healing at the shrine to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, the epidemic ceased, and the festival was started the following year. == Gallery == {{gallery |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_01.jpg|First Torii |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_03.jpg|Shoichii Inari Daimyojin }} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|飛幡八幡宮|version=102018923|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[sv:Tobihata-hachimangū]] [[ja:飛幡八幡宮]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]] {{Commons category|Tobihata Hachiman-gū}} {{wikidata link|Q11665771}} [[Category: [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Tobata-ku, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:History of Tobata Ward]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]]

Hōjōe |prefecture= {{ill|Tobata-ku|WD=Q858143}} |coordinates= {{Coord|33.893333|N|130.827222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Tobihata Hachimangu''' (飛幡八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in [[:en:Tobata-ku, Kitakyūshū|Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu]], {{ill|Kitakyushu|en|Kitakyushu|WD=Q188806|lt=Kitakyushu}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}}. It was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and is currently listed as a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|WD=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|en|Association of Shinto Shrines|WD=Q908077|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Hachiman|en|Hachiman|WD=Q261637|lt=Hachiman}} ({{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū}} and {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}}), {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神|lt=Hime-no-Ōkami|1=Himegami|3=比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, and {{ill|Dōsojin|en|Dōsojin|WD=Q269515|lt=Dōsojin}}. == History == In the Kenkyū period (1190-1199), {{ill|Utsunomiya Shigeie|ja|宇都宮重業|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shigeie|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya clan|1=Utsunomiya clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449780}} ({{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aso clan|qq=}}), brought the Hachiman deity from his hometown of Utsunomiya and enshrined it in Miyata Mountain in Edamitsu Village, which was the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門|1=Kimon|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimon|qq=}} (northeast) of {{ill|Hanao Castle|ja|花尾城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanao Castle|1=Hanao Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615260}}. Later, the shrine was moved to Tobata Village and became the guardian deity of Tobata and Nakahara Villages. During the Edo period, it was designated a prayer site by the {{ill|Kuroda clan|en|Kuroda clan|WD=Q2975305|lt=Kuroda clan}}. In 1920, it was relocated to its current site in Asano. In 1931, it was designated a Prefectural Shrine. In 1995, the shrine's name was changed from "Tobata Hachiman Shrine" to "Tobihata Hachimangu." == festivals == The {{ill|Tobata Gion Yamagasa festival|en|Tobata Gion Yamagasa festival|WD=Q7811554|lt=Tobata Gion Yamakasa}} festival, held on the fourth weekend of July, is a major event at the shrine and at {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu)|ja|菅原神社 (北九州市戸畑区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara Shrine (Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu)|1=Sugawara Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17218762}} and Nakahara Hachiman Shrine in the ward. It is designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} of Japan. According to the {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki|1=Chikuzen'nokuni Zoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108571667}}, the festival began in Kyōwa 2 (1802) when an epidemic broke out in Tobata Village. After praying for healing at the shrine to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, the epidemic ceased, and the festival was started the following year. == Gallery == {{gallery |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_01.jpg|First Torii |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_03.jpg|Shoichii Inari Daimyojin }} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|飛幡八幡宮|version=102018923|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[sv:Tobihata-hachimangū]] [[ja:飛幡八幡宮]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]] {{Commons category|Tobihata Hachiman-gū}} {{wikidata link|Q11665771}} [[Category: [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Tobata-ku, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:History of Tobata Ward]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]]

Hōjōe |prefecture= {{ill|Tobata-ku|WD=Q858143}} |coordinates= {{Coord|33.893333|N|130.827222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Tobihata Hachimangu''' (飛幡八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in [[:en:Tobata-ku, Kitakyūshū|Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu]], {{ill|Kitakyushu|en|Kitakyushu|WD=Q188806|lt=Kitakyushu}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}}. It was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and is currently listed as a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|WD=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|en|Association of Shinto Shrines|WD=Q908077|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Hachiman|en|Hachiman|WD=Q261637|lt=Hachiman}} ({{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū}} and {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}}), {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神|lt=Hime-no-Ōkami|1=Himegami|3=比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, and {{ill|Dōsojin|en|Dōsojin|WD=Q269515|lt=Dōsojin}}. == History == In the Kenkyū period (1190-1199), {{ill|Utsunomiya Shigeie|ja|宇都宮重業|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shigeie|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya clan|1=Utsunomiya clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449780}} ({{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aso clan|qq=}}), brought the Hachiman deity from his hometown of Utsunomiya and enshrined it in Miyata Mountain in Edamitsu Village, which was the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門|1=Kimon|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimon|qq=}} (northeast) of {{ill|Hanao Castle|ja|花尾城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanao Castle|1=Hanao Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615260}}. Later, the shrine was moved to Tobata Village and became the guardian deity of Tobata and Nakahara Villages. During the Edo period, it was designated a prayer site by the {{ill|Kuroda clan|en|Kuroda clan|WD=Q2975305|lt=Kuroda clan}}. In 1920, it was relocated to its current site in Asano. In 1931, it was designated a Prefectural Shrine. In 1995, the shrine's name was changed from "Tobata Hachiman Shrine" to "Tobihata Hachimangu." == festivals == The {{ill|Tobata Gion Yamagasa festival|en|Tobata Gion Yamagasa festival|WD=Q7811554|lt=Tobata Gion Yamakasa}} festival, held on the fourth weekend of July, is a major event at the shrine and at {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu)|ja|菅原神社 (北九州市戸畑区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara Shrine (Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu)|1=Sugawara Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17218762}} and Nakahara Hachiman Shrine in the ward. It is designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} of Japan. According to the {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki|1=Chikuzen'nokuni Zoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108571667}}, the festival began in Kyōwa 2 (1802) when an epidemic broke out in Tobata Village. After praying for healing at the shrine to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, the epidemic ceased, and the festival was started the following year. == Gallery == {{gallery |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_01.jpg|First Torii |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_03.jpg|Shoichii Inari Daimyojin }} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|飛幡八幡宮|version=102018923|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[sv:Tobihata-hachimangū]] [[ja:飛幡八幡宮]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]] {{Commons category|Tobihata Hachiman-gū}} {{wikidata link|Q11665771}} [[Category: [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Tobata-ku, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:History of Tobata Ward]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]] [[Category:pages]]

Hōjōe |prefecture= {{ill|Tobata-ku|WD=Q858143|1=Tobata-ku}} |coordinates= {{Coord|33.893333|N|130.827222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Tobihata Hachimangu''' (飛幡八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in [[:en:Tobata-ku, Kitakyūshū|Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu]], {{ill|Kitakyushu|en|Kitakyushu|WD=Q188806|lt=Kitakyushu|1=Kitakyushu}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}. It was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and is currently listed as a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|WD=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine|1=Beppyo shrine}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|en|Association of Shinto Shrines|WD=Q908077|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines|1=Association of Shinto Shrines}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Hachiman|en|Hachiman|WD=Q261637|lt=Hachiman|1=Hachiman}} ({{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}} and {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}}), {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神|lt=Hime-no-Ōkami|1=Himegami|3=比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, and {{ill|Dōsojin|en|Dōsojin|WD=Q269515|lt=Dōsojin|1=Dōsojin}}. == History == In the Kenkyū period (1190-1199), {{ill|Utsunomiya Shigeie|ja|宇都宮重業|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shigeie|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya clan|1=Utsunomiya clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449780}} ({{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aso clan|qq=}}), brought the Hachiman deity from his hometown of Utsunomiya and enshrined it in Miyata Mountain in Edamitsu Village, which was the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門|1=Kimon|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimon|qq=}} (northeast) of {{ill|Hanao Castle|ja|花尾城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanao Castle|1=Q11615260|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615260}}. Later, the shrine was moved to Tobata Village and became the guardian deity of Tobata and Nakahara Villages. During the Edo period, it was designated a prayer site by the {{ill|Kuroda clan|en|Kuroda clan|WD=Q2975305|lt=Kuroda clan|1=Kuroda clan}}. In 1920, it was relocated to its current site in Asano. In 1931, it was designated a Prefectural Shrine. In 1995, the shrine's name was changed from "Tobata Hachiman Shrine" to "Tobihata Hachimangu." == festivals == The {{ill|Tobata Gion Yamagasa festival|en|Tobata Gion Yamagasa festival|WD=Q7811554|lt=Tobata Gion Yamakasa|1=Tobata Gion Yamagasa festival}} festival, held on the fourth weekend of July, is a major event at the shrine and at {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu)|ja|菅原神社 (北九州市戸畑区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara Shrine (Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu)|1=Sugawara Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17218762}} and Nakahara Hachiman Shrine in the ward. It is designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} of Japan. According to the {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki|1=Chikuzen no Kuni Zoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108571667}}, the festival began in Kyōwa 2 (1802) when an epidemic broke out in Tobata Village. After praying for healing at the shrine to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, the epidemic ceased, and the festival was started the following year. == Gallery == {{gallery |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_01.jpg|First Torii |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_03.jpg|Shoichii Inari Daimyojin }} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|飛幡八幡宮|version=102018923|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[sv:Tobihata-hachimangū]] [[ja:飛幡八幡宮]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]] {{Commons category|Tobihata Hachiman-gū}} {{wikidata link|Q11665771}} [[Category: [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Tobata-ku, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:History of Tobata Ward]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]] [[Category:pages]]

Hōjōe |prefecture= {{ill|Tobata-ku|WD=Q858143|1=Tobata-ku}} |coordinates= {{Coord|33.893333|N|130.827222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Tobihata Hachimangu''' (飛幡八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in [[:en:Tobata-ku, Kitakyūshū|Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu]], {{ill|Kitakyushu|en|Kitakyushu|WD=Q188806|lt=Kitakyushu|1=Kitakyushu}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}. It was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and is currently listed as a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|WD=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine|1=Beppyo shrine}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|en|Association of Shinto Shrines|WD=Q908077|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines|1=Association of Shinto Shrines}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Hachiman|en|Hachiman|WD=Q261637|lt=Hachiman|1=Hachiman}} ({{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}} and {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}}), {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神|lt=Hime-no-Ōkami|1=Himegami|3=比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, and {{ill|Dōsojin|en|Dōsojin|WD=Q269515|lt=Dōsojin|1=Dōsojin}}. == History == In the Kenkyū period (1190-1199), {{ill|Utsunomiya Shigeie|ja|宇都宮重業|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shigeie|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya clan|1=Utsunomiya clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449780}} ({{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aso clan|qq=}}), brought the Hachiman deity from his hometown of Utsunomiya and enshrined it in Miyata Mountain in Edamitsu Village, which was the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門|1=Kimon|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimon|qq=}} (northeast) of {{ill|Hanao Castle|ja|花尾城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanao Castle|1=Q11615260|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615260}}. Later, the shrine was moved to Tobata Village and became the guardian deity of Tobata and Nakahara Villages. During the Edo period, it was designated a prayer site by the {{ill|Kuroda clan|en|Kuroda clan|WD=Q2975305|lt=Kuroda clan|1=Kuroda clan}}. In 1920, it was relocated to its current site in Asano. In 1931, it was designated a Prefectural Shrine. In 1995, the shrine's name was changed from "Tobata Hachiman Shrine" to "Tobihata Hachimangu." == festivals == The {{ill|Tobata Gion Yamagasa festival|en|Tobata Gion Yamagasa festival|WD=Q7811554|lt=Tobata Gion Yamakasa|1=Tobata Gion Yamagasa festival}} festival, held on the fourth weekend of July, is a major event at the shrine and at {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu)|ja|菅原神社 (北九州市戸畑区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara Shrine (Tobata Ward, Kitakyushu)|1=Sugawara Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17218762}} and Nakahara Hachiman Shrine in the ward. It is designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} of Japan. According to the {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki|1=Chikuzen no Kuni Zoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108571667}}, the festival began in Kyōwa 2 (1802) when an epidemic broke out in Tobata Village. After praying for healing at the shrine to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, the epidemic ceased, and the festival was started the following year. == Gallery == {{gallery |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_01.jpg|First Torii |File:Tobihata_Hachimangu_in_Fukuoka_03.jpg|Shoichii Inari Daimyojin }} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|飛幡八幡宮|version=102018923|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[sv:Tobihata-hachimangū]] [[ja:飛幡八幡宮]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Tobihata Hachimangu]] {{Commons category|Tobihata Hachiman-gū}} {{wikidata link|Q11665771}} [[Category: [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tobata-ku, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:History of Tobata Ward]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
